make.description: Allocates and initializes an object of type slice, map, or chan and returns it. For slices, the size specifies the length; a second integer argument may specify the capacity. For maps and channels, the size is optional and hints at initial capacity.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: The type to create (must be a slice, map, or channel type)

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Optional length and capacity for slices, or initial capacity hint for maps and channels

new.description: Allocates zeroed storage for a new item of the given type and returns a pointer to it. The value is initialized to the zero value of the type. It is equivalent to taking the address of a zero-valued variable.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The type for which to allocate memory

append.description: Appends elements to the end of a slice and returns the resulting slice. If the backing array is too small, a new array is allocated and the existing elements are copied over. The special form append([]byte, string...) appends the bytes of a string.

parameters.name: slice
parameters.description: The slice to append to

parameters.name: elems
parameters.description: The elements to append to the slice

copy.description: Copies elements from a source slice into a destination slice and returns the number of elements copied. The number copied is the minimum of len(src) and len(dst). As a special case, copy also accepts a destination of type []byte and a source of type string.

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: The destination slice to copy into

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: The source slice to copy from

delete.description: Deletes the element with the specified key from the map. If the map is nil or no such element exists, delete is a no-op. It does not return any value.

parameters.name: m
parameters.description: The map from which to delete the element

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The key of the element to delete

len.description: Returns the length of a value according to its type: the number of elements in an array, slice, map, string (bytes), or channel buffer. For nil slices, maps, and channels, len returns 0.

parameters.name: v
parameters.description: The value whose length to return (array, slice, map, string, or channel)

cap.description: Returns the capacity of a value according to its type: the maximum length a slice can reach, the buffer capacity of a channel, or the length of an array. For nil slices and channels, cap returns 0.

parameters.name: v
parameters.description: The value whose capacity to return (array, slice, or channel)

close.description: Closes a channel, indicating that no more values will be sent on it. Receiving from a closed channel returns the zero value without blocking. Closing a nil channel or an already-closed channel causes a panic.

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: The channel to close (must be bidirectional or send-only)

panic.description: Stops normal execution of the current goroutine and begins panicking. Deferred functions are still executed as the stack unwinds. If the panic is not recovered, the program terminates with a stack trace and the value passed to panic.

parameters.name: v
parameters.description: The value to pass to the panic, typically an error or string

recover.description: Allows a program to manage behavior of a panicking goroutine. Calling recover inside a deferred function stops the panicking sequence and returns the value passed to the call of panic. If called outside a deferred function or when the goroutine is not panicking, recover returns nil.

complex.description: Constructs a complex value from two floating-point values representing the real and imaginary parts. Both arguments must have the same floating-point type (float32 or float64), and the result is complex64 or complex128 respectively.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: The real part of the complex number

parameters.name: i
parameters.description: The imaginary part of the complex number

real.description: Returns the real part of a complex number. If the argument is complex64, the return type is float32. If the argument is complex128, the return type is float64.

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: The complex number from which to extract the real part

imag.description: Returns the imaginary part of a complex number. If the argument is complex64, the return type is float32. If the argument is complex128, the return type is float64.

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: The complex number from which to extract the imaginary part

clear.description: Clears maps and slices. For maps, clear deletes all entries, resulting in an empty map. For slices, clear sets all elements up to the length to the zero value of the element type. If the argument is a type parameter, all types in its type set must be maps or slices.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: The map or slice to clear

min.signature: func min[T cmp.Ordered](x T, y ...T) T
min.description: Returns the smallest value among its arguments. Arguments must be ordered types (integers, floats, or strings). For floating-point arguments, min follows IEEE 754 rules: min(-0.0, 0.0) returns -0.0, and min propagates NaN.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The first value to compare

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Additional values to compare

max.signature: func max[T cmp.Ordered](x T, y ...T) T
max.description: Returns the largest value among its arguments. Arguments must be ordered types (integers, floats, or strings). For floating-point arguments, max follows IEEE 754 rules: max(-0.0, 0.0) returns 0.0, and max propagates NaN.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The first value to compare

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Additional values to compare

print.description: Formats its arguments in an implementation-specific way and writes the result to standard error. Spaces are added between arguments. It is useful for bootstrapping and debugging but is not guaranteed to remain in the language.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: The values to print to standard error

println.description: Formats its arguments in an implementation-specific way and writes the result followed by a newline to standard error. Spaces are added between arguments. Useful for bootstrapping and debugging but not guaranteed to remain in the language.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: The values to print to standard error followed by a newline

error.description: The built-in error interface type. The conventional interface for representing an error condition, with the nil value representing no error. Most functions that can fail return an error as their last return value.

string.description: The built-in string type represents the set of all strings of 8-bit bytes, conventionally but not necessarily representing UTF-8-encoded text. A string may be empty but not nil. Values of type string are immutable.

int.description: A signed integer type that is at least 32 bits in size. It is a distinct type, however, and not an alias for, say, int32. The size is implementation-specific: 32 bits on 32-bit systems and 64 bits on 64-bit systems.

int8.description: The set of all signed 8-bit integers, ranging from -128 to 127. It occupies exactly 1 byte of memory.

int16.description: The set of all signed 16-bit integers, ranging from -32768 to 32767. It occupies exactly 2 bytes of memory.

int32.description: The set of all signed 32-bit integers, ranging from -2147483648 to 2147483647. It occupies exactly 4 bytes of memory. It is also aliased as rune.

int64.description: The set of all signed 64-bit integers, ranging from -9223372036854775808 to 9223372036854775807. It occupies exactly 8 bytes of memory.

uint.description: An unsigned integer type that is the same size as int. It is at least 32 bits in size, and its exact size is implementation-specific: 32 bits on 32-bit systems, 64 bits on 64-bit systems.

uint8.description: The set of all unsigned 8-bit integers, ranging from 0 to 255. It occupies exactly 1 byte of memory. It is also aliased as byte.

uint16.description: The set of all unsigned 16-bit integers, ranging from 0 to 65535. It occupies exactly 2 bytes of memory.

uint32.description: The set of all unsigned 32-bit integers, ranging from 0 to 4294967295. It occupies exactly 4 bytes of memory.

uint64.description: The set of all unsigned 64-bit integers, ranging from 0 to 18446744073709551615. It occupies exactly 8 bytes of memory.

float32.description: The set of all IEEE-754 32-bit floating-point numbers. It provides approximately 6-7 decimal digits of precision and occupies 4 bytes of memory.

float64.description: The set of all IEEE-754 64-bit floating-point numbers. It provides approximately 15-17 decimal digits of precision and occupies 8 bytes of memory. It is the default floating-point type in Go.

complex64.description: The set of all complex numbers with float32 real and imaginary parts. It occupies 8 bytes of memory total (4 bytes for each component).

complex128.description: The set of all complex numbers with float64 real and imaginary parts. It occupies 16 bytes of memory total (8 bytes for each component).

bool.description: The set of boolean values: true and false. The zero value of bool is false. Booleans are used in conditional expressions and logical operations.

byte.description: An alias for uint8 and is equivalent to uint8 in all ways. It is used by convention to distinguish byte values from 8-bit unsigned integer values. Commonly used when working with raw data and strings.

rune.description: An alias for int32 and is equivalent to int32 in all ways. It is used by convention to represent a Unicode code point. A rune literal is a single character enclosed in single quotes.

any.description: An alias for interface{}. Introduced in Go 1.18 for use with generics. Can be used as a type constraint meaning 'any type'.

comparable.description: An interface implemented by all comparable types (booleans, numbers, strings, pointers, channels, arrays of comparable types, structs with comparable fields). Used as a type constraint.

uintptr.description: An unsigned integer type that is large enough to hold the bit pattern of any pointer. It is used for unsafe pointer arithmetic and interoperation with C or system calls. Unlike unsafe.Pointer, a uintptr is just a number and does not keep the referenced object alive.

fmt.Println.description: Formats using the default formats for its operands and writes to standard output. Spaces are always added between operands and a newline is appended. Returns the number of bytes written and any write error encountered.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: The values to print, each formatted with its default format

fmt.Printf.signature: func fmt.Printf(format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
fmt.Printf.description: Formats according to a format specifier string and writes to standard output. Returns the number of bytes written and any write error encountered. The format string uses verbs like %d, %s, %v, etc.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: A format string containing verbs like %d, %s, %v

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: The values to format according to the format specifier

fmt.Sprintf.description: Formats according to a format specifier and returns the resulting string. It does not write to any output; instead it returns the formatted string. Uses the same verb syntax as fmt.Printf.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: A format string containing verbs like %d, %s, %v

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: The values to format according to the format specifier

fmt.Fprintf.signature: func fmt.Fprintf(w io.Writer, format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
fmt.Fprintf.description: Formats according to a format specifier and writes to the specified io.Writer. Returns the number of bytes written and any write error encountered. Commonly used to write to files, buffers, or network connections.

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: The writer to write the formatted output to

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: A format string containing verbs like %d, %s, %v

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: The values to format according to the format specifier

fmt.Errorf.description: Formats according to a format specifier and returns the string as a value that satisfies the error interface. If the format specifier includes a %w verb with an error operand, the returned error will implement Unwrap() to return that operand.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: A format string; use %w to wrap an error value

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: The values to format, including an error for %w wrapping

fmt.Sscanf.signature: func fmt.Sscanf(str string, format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
fmt.Sscanf.description: Scans the argument string, storing successive space-separated values into successive arguments as determined by the format. Returns the number of items successfully scanned. Newlines in the input must match newlines in the format.

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: The input string to scan

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: A format string specifying how to scan the input

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Pointers to variables where scanned values will be stored

fmt.Fscanf.signature: func fmt.Fscanf(r io.Reader, format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
fmt.Fscanf.description: Scans text read from the io.Reader, storing successive space-separated values into successive arguments as determined by the format. Returns the number of items successfully scanned. Newlines in the input must match newlines in the format.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: The reader to read input from

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: A format string specifying how to scan the input

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Pointers to variables where scanned values will be stored

fmt.Fprintln.signature: func fmt.Fprintln(w io.Writer, a ...any) (n int, err error)
fmt.Fprintln.description: Formats using the default formats for its operands and writes to the specified io.Writer. Spaces are always added between operands and a newline is appended. Returns the number of bytes written and any write error encountered.

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: The writer to write the output to

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: The values to print with default formatting

fmt.Fprint.signature: func fmt.Fprint(w io.Writer, a ...any) (n int, err error)
fmt.Fprint.description: Formats using the default formats for its operands and writes to the specified io.Writer. Spaces are added between operands when neither is a string. Returns the number of bytes written and any write error encountered.

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: The writer to write the output to

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: The values to print with default formatting

fmt.Sprint.description: Formats using the default formats for its operands and returns the resulting string. Spaces are added between operands when neither is a string. Returns the concatenated string representation of all arguments.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: The values to format with default formatting

fmt.Sprintln.description: Formats using the default formats for its operands and returns the resulting string. Spaces are always added between operands and a newline is appended. Useful for building strings with the same formatting as Println.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: The values to format with default formatting

fmt.Scan.description: Scans text read from standard input, storing successive space-separated values into successive arguments. Newlines count as spaces. Returns the number of items successfully scanned. If fewer items are scanned than arguments provided, an error is returned.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Pointers to variables where scanned values will be stored

fmt.Scanln.description: Scans text read from standard input, storing successive space-separated values into successive arguments. Scanning stops at a newline, and after the last item there must be a newline or EOF. Returns the number of items successfully scanned.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Pointers to variables where scanned values will be stored

fmt.Scanf.signature: func fmt.Scanf(format string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
fmt.Scanf.description: Scans text read from standard input, storing successive values as determined by the format into successive arguments. Returns the number of items successfully scanned. Newlines in the input must match newlines in the format string.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: A format string specifying how to scan the input

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Pointers to variables where scanned values will be stored

fmt.Sscan.signature: func fmt.Sscan(str string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
fmt.Sscan.description: Scans the argument string, storing successive space-separated values into successive arguments. Newlines count as spaces. Returns the number of items successfully scanned. If fewer items are read than provided, an error is returned.

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: The input string to scan

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Pointers to variables where scanned values will be stored

fmt.Sscanln.signature: func fmt.Sscanln(str string, a ...any) (n int, err error)
fmt.Sscanln.description: Scans the argument string, storing successive space-separated values into successive arguments. Scanning stops at a newline, and after the last item there must be a newline or EOF. Returns the number of items successfully scanned.

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: The input string to scan

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Pointers to variables where scanned values will be stored

fmt.Stringer.description: An interface implemented by any value that has a String method, which defines the native format for that value. The String method is used by the fmt package when printing values with %s or %v verbs, or when using Println and similar functions.

fmt.GoStringer.description: An interface implemented by any value that has a GoString method, which defines the Go syntax representation for that value. The GoString method is used by the fmt package when printing values with the %#v verb.

fmt.Formatter.signature: type Formatter interface { Format(f State, verb rune) }
fmt.Formatter.description: An interface implemented by any value that has a Format method. The Format method provides full control over formatting and is called by the fmt package for any value that implements it, allowing custom formatting behavior for all verbs.

errors.New.description: Returns an error that formats as the given text. Each call to New returns a distinct error value even if the text is identical. This is the simplest way to create an error in Go.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The error message text

errors.Is.description: Reports whether any error in err's chain matches target. An error's chain consists of err itself followed by the sequence of errors obtained by repeatedly calling Unwrap. An error matches target if it is equal to it or implements Is(error) bool returning true.

parameters.name: err
parameters.description: The error to examine

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The target error to compare against in the chain

errors.As.signature: func errors.As(err error, target any) bool
errors.As.description: Finds the first error in err's chain that matches target, and if so, sets target to that error value and returns true. The target must be a non-nil pointer to either a type that implements error, or to any interface type. It uses type assertions to match.

parameters.name: err
parameters.description: The error to examine

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: A pointer to the target type to set if a match is found

errors.Unwrap.description: Returns the result of calling the Unwrap method on err, if err's type implements Unwrap() error. Otherwise, Unwrap returns nil. Unwrap only calls a method of the form Unwrap() error; it does not unwrap errors returned by Unwrap() []error.

parameters.name: err
parameters.description: The error to unwrap

errors.Join.description: Returns an error that wraps the given errors. Any nil error values are discarded. If every value in errs is nil, Join returns nil. The resulting error formats as the concatenation of the strings obtained by calling Error on each non-nil element, separated by newlines.

parameters.name: errs
parameters.description: The errors to join together

strings.Contains.description: Reports whether substr is within s. Returns true if substr is found anywhere in s, including if substr is the empty string. It is case-sensitive.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to search within

parameters.name: substr
parameters.description: The substring to search for

strings.ContainsAny.description: Reports whether any Unicode code points in chars are within s.

strings.ContainsRune.signature: func strings.ContainsRune(s string, r rune) bool
strings.ContainsRune.description: Reports whether the Unicode code point r is within s.

strings.Count.description: Counts the number of non-overlapping instances of substr in s. If substr is an empty string, Count returns 1 + the number of Unicode code points in s. The search is case-sensitive.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to search within

parameters.name: substr
parameters.description: The substring to count occurrences of

strings.Cut.signature: func strings.Cut(s, sep string) (before, after string, found bool)
strings.Cut.description: Slices s around the first instance of sep, returning the text before and after sep. If sep does not appear in s, Cut returns s, an empty string, and false. Cut is often a simpler alternative to Index and Split.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to cut

parameters.name: sep
parameters.description: The separator to cut around

strings.CutPrefix.signature: func strings.CutPrefix(s, prefix string) (after string, found bool)
strings.CutPrefix.description: Returns s without the provided leading prefix string and reports whether it found the prefix. If s doesn't start with prefix, CutPrefix returns s and false. If prefix is the empty string, CutPrefix returns s and true.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to remove the prefix from

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: The prefix to remove

strings.CutSuffix.signature: func strings.CutSuffix(s, suffix string) (before string, found bool)
strings.CutSuffix.description: Returns s without the provided ending suffix string and reports whether it found the suffix. If s doesn't end with suffix, CutSuffix returns s and false. If suffix is the empty string, CutSuffix returns s and true.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to remove the suffix from

parameters.name: suffix
parameters.description: The suffix to remove

strings.EqualFold.description: Reports whether s and t, interpreted as UTF-8 strings, are equal under simple Unicode case-folding, which is a more general form of case-insensitivity. This function is preferred over ToLower comparison for internationalized text.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The first string to compare

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: The second string to compare

strings.Fields.description: Splits the string s around each instance of one or more consecutive white space characters as defined by unicode.IsSpace. Returns a slice of substrings of s, or an empty slice if s contains only white space.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to split into fields

strings.HasPrefix.description: Tests whether the string s begins with prefix. Returns true if s starts with the given prefix string. An empty prefix always returns true.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to test

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: The prefix to check for

strings.HasSuffix.description: Tests whether the string s ends with suffix. Returns true if s ends with the given suffix string. An empty suffix always returns true.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to test

parameters.name: suffix
parameters.description: The suffix to check for

strings.Index.description: Returns the index of the first instance of substr in s, or -1 if substr is not present in s. The index is the byte position, not the rune position.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to search within

parameters.name: substr
parameters.description: The substring to find

strings.IndexByte.signature: func strings.IndexByte(s string, c byte) int
strings.IndexByte.description: Returns the index of the first instance of c in s, or -1 if not present.

strings.IndexRune.signature: func strings.IndexRune(s string, r rune) int
strings.IndexRune.description: Returns the index of the first instance of the Unicode code point r in s, or -1 if not present.

strings.IndexFunc.signature: func strings.IndexFunc(s string, f func(rune) bool) int
strings.IndexFunc.description: Returns the index into s of the first Unicode code point satisfying f(c), or -1 if none do.

strings.Join.description: Concatenates the elements of its first argument to create a single string. The separator string sep is placed between elements in the resulting string. If elems is empty, Join returns an empty string.

parameters.name: elems
parameters.description: The string slice to join

parameters.name: sep
parameters.description: The separator to place between elements

strings.LastIndex.description: Returns the index of the last instance of substr in s, or -1 if not present.

strings.Map.signature: func strings.Map(mapping func(rune) rune, s string) string
strings.Map.description: Returns a copy of the string s with all its characters modified according to the mapping function. If mapping returns a negative value, the character is dropped from the string with no replacement.

parameters.name: mapping
parameters.description: A function that maps each rune to a replacement rune

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to transform

strings.NewReader.description: Returns a new Reader reading from s. It implements io.Reader, io.ReaderAt, io.ByteReader, io.ByteScanner, io.RuneReader, io.RuneScanner, io.Seeker, and io.WriterTo interfaces. It is similar to bytes.NewReader.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to create a reader from

strings.NewReplacer.description: Returns a new Replacer from a list of old, new string pairs. Replacements are performed in the order they appear in the target string, without overlapping matches. The old string comparisons are done in argument order. Panics if given an odd number of arguments.

parameters.name: oldnew
parameters.description: Pairs of old and new strings for replacement

strings.Repeat.signature: func strings.Repeat(s string, count int) string
strings.Repeat.description: Returns a new string consisting of count copies of the string s. Panics if count is negative or if the result of (len(s) * count) overflows. If count is zero, returns an empty string.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to repeat

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: The number of times to repeat the string

strings.Replace.signature: func strings.Replace(s, old, new string, n int) string
strings.Replace.description: Returns a copy of the string s with the first n non-overlapping instances of old replaced by new. If old is empty, it matches at the beginning of the string and after each UTF-8 sequence. If n < 0, there is no limit on the number of replacements.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The source string

parameters.name: old
parameters.description: The substring to replace

parameters.name: new
parameters.description: The replacement string

parameters.name: n
parameters.description: Maximum number of replacements (-1 for unlimited)

strings.ReplaceAll.signature: func strings.ReplaceAll(s, old, new string) string
strings.ReplaceAll.description: Returns a copy of the string s with all non-overlapping instances of old replaced by new. It is equivalent to Replace with n=-1. If old is empty, it matches at the beginning and after each UTF-8 sequence.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The source string

parameters.name: old
parameters.description: The substring to replace

parameters.name: new
parameters.description: The replacement string

strings.Split.description: Slices s into all substrings separated by sep and returns a slice of the substrings between those separators. If sep is empty, Split splits after each UTF-8 sequence. It is equivalent to SplitN with a count of -1.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to split

parameters.name: sep
parameters.description: The separator to split on

strings.SplitN.signature: func strings.SplitN(s, sep string, n int) []string
strings.SplitN.description: Slices s into substrings separated by sep. n determines the number of substrings to return: n < 0 means no limit.

strings.SplitAfter.description: Slices s into substrings after each instance of sep.

strings.Title.description: Returns a copy with all Unicode letters that begin words mapped to their title case. Deprecated: use golang.org/x/text/cases instead.

strings.ToLower.description: Returns s with all Unicode letters mapped to their lower case. It handles Unicode case mapping rules and returns a new string with all characters converted to lowercase.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to convert to lowercase

strings.ToUpper.description: Returns s with all Unicode letters mapped to their upper case. It handles Unicode case mapping rules and returns a new string with all characters converted to uppercase.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to convert to uppercase

strings.ToTitle.description: Returns a copy with all Unicode letters mapped to their title case.

strings.Trim.description: Returns a slice of the string s with all leading and trailing Unicode code points contained in cutset removed. If cutset is empty, the original string is returned unchanged.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to trim

parameters.name: cutset
parameters.description: The set of characters to remove from both ends

strings.TrimLeft.description: Returns a slice with all leading Unicode code points contained in cutset removed.

strings.TrimRight.description: Returns a slice with all trailing Unicode code points contained in cutset removed.

strings.TrimPrefix.description: Returns s without the provided leading prefix. If s doesn't start with prefix, s is returned unchanged.

strings.TrimSuffix.description: Returns s without the provided trailing suffix. If s doesn't end with suffix, s is returned unchanged.

strings.TrimSpace.description: Returns a slice of the string s, with all leading and trailing white space removed, as defined by Unicode. This includes spaces, tabs, newlines, and other Unicode whitespace characters.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to trim whitespace from

strings.TrimFunc.signature: func strings.TrimFunc(s string, f func(rune) bool) string
strings.TrimFunc.description: Returns a slice with all leading and trailing Unicode code points c satisfying f(c) removed.

strings.Builder.description: A Builder is used to efficiently build a string using Write methods. It minimizes memory copying by using an internal byte slice that grows as needed. A zero-value Builder is ready to use. Do not copy a non-zero Builder.

strings.Clone.description: Returns a fresh copy of s. Guarantees the returned string's memory is newly allocated. Added in Go 1.20.

strconv.Atoi.description: Converts a string to an int. It is equivalent to ParseInt(s, 10, 0) converted to type int. Returns the converted integer and an error if the string is not a valid integer or is out of range.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to convert to an integer

strconv.Itoa.description: Converts an int to its decimal string representation. It is equivalent to FormatInt(int64(i), 10). It is the inverse of Atoi.

parameters.name: i
parameters.description: The integer to convert to a string

strconv.ParseInt.signature: func strconv.ParseInt(s string, base int, bitSize int) (int64, error)
strconv.ParseInt.description: Interprets a string s in the given base (0, 2 to 36) and bit size (0 to 64) and returns the corresponding int64 value. If the base argument is 0, the true base is implied by the string's prefix: 0b for binary, 0o or 0 for octal, 0x for hex, otherwise decimal.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to parse

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: The numeric base (0, or 2 to 36); 0 means infer from prefix

parameters.name: bitSize
parameters.description: The integer bit size (0, 8, 16, 32, or 64)

strconv.ParseFloat.signature: func strconv.ParseFloat(s string, bitSize int) (float64, error)
strconv.ParseFloat.description: Converts the string s to a floating-point number with the precision specified by bitSize: 32 for float32, or 64 for float64. When bitSize is 32, the result still has type float64 but will be convertible to float32 without changing its value.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to parse as a floating-point number

parameters.name: bitSize
parameters.description: The floating-point precision (32 or 64)

strconv.ParseBool.description: Returns the boolean value represented by the string. It accepts 1, t, T, TRUE, true, True, 0, f, F, FALSE, false, False. Any other value returns an error.

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: The string to parse as a boolean

strconv.ParseUint.signature: func strconv.ParseUint(s string, base int, bitSize int) (uint64, error)
strconv.ParseUint.description: Is like ParseInt but for unsigned numbers. It interprets a string s in the given base (0, 2 to 36) and bit size (0 to 64) and returns the corresponding uint64 value. It does not accept sign prefixes.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to parse

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: The numeric base (0, or 2 to 36); 0 means infer from prefix

parameters.name: bitSize
parameters.description: The integer bit size (0, 8, 16, 32, or 64)

strconv.FormatInt.signature: func strconv.FormatInt(i int64, base int) string
strconv.FormatInt.description: Returns the string representation of i in the given base, for 2 <= base <= 36. The result uses the lower-case letters 'a' to 'z' for digit values >= 10.

parameters.name: i
parameters.description: The integer to format

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: The numeric base (2 to 36)

strconv.FormatFloat.signature: func strconv.FormatFloat(f float64, fmt byte, prec, bitSize int) string
strconv.FormatFloat.description: Converts the floating-point number f to a string according to the format fmt and precision prec. The format fmt is one of 'b', 'e', 'E', 'f', 'g', 'G', 'x', 'X'. The prec controls the number of digits; -1 uses the smallest number needed for exact representation.

parameters.name: f
parameters.description: The floating-point number to format

parameters.name: fmt
parameters.description: The format specifier ('b', 'e', 'E', 'f', 'g', 'G', 'x', 'X')

parameters.name: prec
parameters.description: The precision (number of digits); -1 for smallest exact representation

parameters.name: bitSize
parameters.description: The original bit size (32 or 64) to round correctly

strconv.FormatBool.description: Returns "true" or "false" according to the value of b. It is the inverse of ParseBool for the canonical true/false string representations.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: The boolean value to format

strconv.FormatUint.signature: func strconv.FormatUint(i uint64, base int) string
strconv.FormatUint.description: Returns the string representation of i in the given base, for 2 <= base <= 36. The result uses the lower-case letters 'a' to 'z' for digit values >= 10. It is the unsigned equivalent of FormatInt.

parameters.name: i
parameters.description: The unsigned integer to format

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: The numeric base (2 to 36)

strconv.Quote.description: Returns a double-quoted Go string literal representing s. The returned string uses Go escape sequences (\t, \n, \xFF, \u0100) for control characters and non-printable characters as defined by IsPrint.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to quote

strconv.Unquote.description: Interprets s as a single-quoted, double-quoted, or backquoted Go string literal and returns the string value that s quotes. If s is single-quoted, it must be a valid Go rune literal. Returns an error if the input is not a valid quoted string.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The quoted string to unquote

strconv.QuoteRune.description: Returns a single-quoted Go character literal representing the rune. The returned string uses Go escape sequences for control characters and non-printable characters as defined by IsPrint.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: The rune to quote

strconv.AppendInt.signature: func strconv.AppendInt(dst []byte, i int64, base int) []byte
strconv.AppendInt.description: Appends the string form of the integer i, as generated by FormatInt, to dst and returns the extended buffer. It is more efficient than FormatInt when building byte slices incrementally.

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: The byte slice to append to

parameters.name: i
parameters.description: The integer to format and append

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: The numeric base (2 to 36)

strconv.AppendFloat.signature: func strconv.AppendFloat(dst []byte, f float64, fmt byte, prec, bitSize int) []byte
strconv.AppendFloat.description: Appends the string form of the floating-point number f, as generated by FormatFloat, to dst and returns the extended buffer. It is more efficient than FormatFloat when building byte slices incrementally.

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: The byte slice to append to

parameters.name: f
parameters.description: The floating-point number to format and append

parameters.name: fmt
parameters.description: The format specifier ('b', 'e', 'E', 'f', 'g', 'G', 'x', 'X')

parameters.name: prec
parameters.description: The precision (number of digits)

parameters.name: bitSize
parameters.description: The original bit size (32 or 64)

strconv.CanBackquote.description: Reports whether the string s can be represented unchanged as a single-line backquoted string with no control characters other than tab. Returns false if the string contains any control characters (except tab), backquotes, or invalid UTF-8 sequences.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to check

os.Open.description: Opens the named file for reading. If successful, methods on the returned File can be used for reading; the associated file descriptor has mode O_RDONLY. If there is an error, it will be of type *PathError.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The path to the file to open

os.Create.description: Creates or truncates the named file. If the file already exists, it is truncated. If the file does not exist, it is created with mode 0666 (before umask). The returned File is open for reading and writing.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The path to the file to create

os.OpenFile.signature: func os.OpenFile(name string, flag int, perm os.FileMode) (*os.File, error)
os.OpenFile.description: Opens the named file with specified flag (O_RDONLY etc.) and permissions. It is the generalized open call; most users will use Open or Create instead. If there is an error, it will be of type *PathError.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The path to the file to open

parameters.name: flag
parameters.description: File open flags such as os.O_RDONLY, os.O_WRONLY, os.O_CREATE, etc.

parameters.name: perm
parameters.description: Unix permission bits to use when creating the file

os.ReadFile.description: Reads the named file and returns the contents. A successful call returns err == nil, not err == io.EOF. It reads the entire file into memory, so it should not be used for very large files.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The path to the file to read

os.WriteFile.signature: func os.WriteFile(name string, data []byte, perm os.FileMode) error
os.WriteFile.description: Writes data to the named file, creating it if necessary. If the file does not exist, WriteFile creates it with permissions perm (before umask); otherwise it truncates it before writing, without changing permissions.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The path to the file to write

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The byte slice to write to the file

parameters.name: perm
parameters.description: Unix permission bits for the file if created

os.Stat.description: Returns a FileInfo describing the named file. If there is an error, it will be of type *PathError. It follows symbolic links; to get info about a link itself, use Lstat.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The path to the file or directory

os.Lstat.description: Returns the FileInfo for the named file. If the file is a symbolic link, returns info about the link itself, not the target.

os.Remove.description: Removes the named file or empty directory. If there is an error, it will be of type *PathError. It does not remove non-empty directories; use RemoveAll for that.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The path to the file or empty directory to remove

os.RemoveAll.description: Removes path and any children it contains. It removes everything it can but returns the first error it encounters. If the path does not exist, RemoveAll returns nil (no error).

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The path to remove recursively

os.Mkdir.signature: func os.Mkdir(name string, perm os.FileMode) error
os.Mkdir.description: Creates a new directory with the specified name and permission bits (before umask). If there is an error, it will be of type *PathError. The parent directory must already exist.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The path of the directory to create

parameters.name: perm
parameters.description: Permission bits for the new directory

os.MkdirAll.signature: func os.MkdirAll(path string, perm os.FileMode) error
os.MkdirAll.description: Creates a directory named path, along with any necessary parents, and returns nil, or else returns an error. The permission bits perm are used for all directories that MkdirAll creates. If path is already a directory, MkdirAll does nothing and returns nil.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The full directory path to create

parameters.name: perm
parameters.description: Permission bits for any created directories

os.Rename.description: Renames (moves) oldpath to newpath. If newpath already exists and is not a directory, Rename replaces it. OS-specific restrictions may apply when oldpath and newpath are in different directories.

parameters.name: oldpath
parameters.description: The current path of the file or directory

parameters.name: newpath
parameters.description: The new path for the file or directory

os.Getwd.description: Returns a rooted path name corresponding to the current working directory. If the current directory can be reached via multiple paths (due to symbolic links), Getwd may return any one of them.

os.Chdir.description: Changes the current working directory to the named directory. If there is an error, it will be of type *PathError.

parameters.name: dir
parameters.description: The directory to change to

os.Chmod.signature: func os.Chmod(name string, mode os.FileMode) error
os.Chmod.description: Changes the mode of the named file to mode.

os.Chown.signature: func os.Chown(name string, uid, gid int) error
os.Chown.description: Changes the numeric uid and gid of the named file.

os.Symlink.description: Creates newname as a symbolic link to oldname.

os.Readlink.description: Returns the destination of the named symbolic link.

os.ReadDir.description: Reads the named directory, returning all its directory entries sorted by filename.

os.TempDir.description: Returns the default directory to use for temporary files. On Unix, it returns $TMPDIR if non-empty, else /tmp. On Windows, it uses GetTempPath, returning the first non-empty value from %TMP%, %TEMP%, %USERPROFILE%, or the Windows directory.

os.CreateTemp.description: Creates a new temporary file in the directory dir, opens the file for reading and writing, and returns the resulting file. The filename is generated by taking pattern and adding a random string to the end. If pattern includes a '*', the random string replaces the last '*'.

os.MkdirTemp.description: Creates a new temporary directory in the directory dir and returns the pathname of the new directory.

os.Exit.description: Causes the current program to exit with the given status code. Conventionally, code zero indicates success, non-zero an error. The program terminates immediately; deferred functions are not run. For portability, status codes should be in the range [0, 125].

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: The exit status code

os.Getenv.description: Retrieves the value of the environment variable named by the key. It returns the value, which will be empty if the variable is not present. To distinguish between an empty value and an unset value, use LookupEnv.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The name of the environment variable

os.Setenv.description: Sets the value of the environment variable named by the key. If the key is invalid or the value contains a NUL byte, it returns an error. It affects the current process and any child processes.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The name of the environment variable

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to set

os.LookupEnv.description: Retrieves the value of the environment variable named by the key. If the variable is present in the environment, the value (which may be empty) is returned and the boolean is true. Otherwise the returned value will be empty and the boolean will be false.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The name of the environment variable

os.Environ.description: Returns a copy of strings representing the environment, in the form key=value.

os.ExpandEnv.description: Replaces ${var} or $var in the string according to the values of the current environment variables. References to undefined variables are replaced by the empty string.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string containing environment variable references to expand

os.Hostname.description: Returns the host name reported by the kernel.

os.Getpid.description: Returns the process id of the caller.

os.Getppid.description: Returns the process id of the caller's parent. This is the operating system's identifier for the parent process that started the current process.

os.Getuid.description: Returns the numeric user id of the caller. On Windows, returns -1.

os.Getgid.description: Returns the numeric group id of the caller. On Windows, it returns -1 as the concept of Unix group IDs does not apply.

os.Executable.description: Returns the path name for the executable that started the current process. There is no guarantee that the path is still pointing to the correct executable.

os.UserCacheDir.description: Returns the default root directory to use for user-specific cached data. On Unix it returns $XDG_CACHE_HOME or $HOME/.cache, on Darwin $HOME/Library/Caches, and on Windows %LocalAppData%.

os.UserConfigDir.description: Returns the default root directory to use for user-specific configuration data. On Unix it returns $XDG_CONFIG_HOME or $HOME/.config, on Darwin $HOME/Library/Application Support, and on Windows %AppData%.

os.UserHomeDir.description: Returns the current user's home directory. On Unix it returns the $HOME environment variable. On Windows it returns %USERPROFILE%. On Plan 9 it returns the $home environment variable.

os.Pipe.description: Returns a connected pair of Files; reads from r return bytes written to w. It returns the files and an error, if any.

os.IsNotExist.description: Reports whether err is known to report that a file or directory does not exist. Prefer errors.Is(err, fs.ErrNotExist).

os.IsExist.description: Reports whether err is known to report that a file or directory already exists.

os.IsPermission.description: Reports whether err is known to report a permission denied error.

os.Args.description: Args holds the command-line arguments, starting with the program name. It is set by the runtime before main runs and is equivalent to argv in C programs.

os.Stdin.description: Stdin is an open File pointing to the standard input file descriptor. It is connected to the process's standard input stream for reading user or piped input.

os.Stdout.description: Stdout is an open File pointing to the standard output file descriptor. It is the default destination for fmt.Print and related functions.

os.Stderr.description: Stderr is an open File pointing to the standard error file descriptor. It is used for writing diagnostic output separately from standard output.

io.Reader.signature: type io.Reader interface { Read(p []byte) (n int, err error) }
io.Reader.description: Reader is the interface that wraps the basic Read method. Read reads up to len(p) bytes into p, returning the number of bytes read and any error encountered. Implementations must not retain p after returning.

io.Writer.signature: type io.Writer interface { Write(p []byte) (n int, err error) }
io.Writer.description: Writer is the interface that wraps the basic Write method. Write writes len(p) bytes from p to the underlying data stream, returning the number of bytes written and any error. Write must not modify the slice data, even temporarily.

io.Closer.description: Closer is the interface that wraps the basic Close method. The behavior of Close after the first call is undefined. Specific implementations may document their own behavior.

io.ReadCloser.description: ReadCloser is the interface that groups the basic Read and Close methods. It is commonly returned by functions that open a resource for reading and require the caller to close it when done.

io.WriteCloser.description: WriteCloser is the interface that groups the basic Write and Close methods. It is commonly returned by functions that open a resource for writing and require the caller to close it when done.

io.ReadWriter.description: ReadWriter is the interface that groups the basic Read and Write methods. Types that implement both Reader and Writer satisfy this interface automatically.

io.ReadWriteCloser.signature: type io.ReadWriteCloser interface { Reader; Writer; Closer }
io.ReadWriteCloser.description: ReadWriteCloser is the interface that groups the basic Read, Write, and Close methods. It combines full bidirectional I/O with the ability to release the underlying resource.

io.Seeker.signature: type io.Seeker interface { Seek(offset int64, whence int) (int64, error) }
io.Seeker.description: The interface that wraps the basic Seek method. whence constants: io.SeekStart(0), io.SeekCurrent(1), io.SeekEnd(2).

io.ReadSeeker.description: ReadSeeker is the interface that groups the basic Read and Seek methods. It allows both sequential reading and random access within a data source.

io.ReaderAt.signature: type io.ReaderAt interface { ReadAt(p []byte, off int64) (n int, err error) }
io.ReaderAt.description: The interface for reading at a specific offset. ReadAt reads len(p) bytes starting at byte offset off.

io.WriterTo.signature: type io.WriterTo interface { WriteTo(w Writer) (n int64, err error) }
io.WriterTo.description: The interface that wraps the WriteTo method. Used for efficient copying without intermediate buffers.

io.ReaderFrom.signature: type io.ReaderFrom interface { ReadFrom(r Reader) (n int64, err error) }
io.ReaderFrom.description: The interface that wraps the ReadFrom method.

io.ReadAll.description: Reads from r until an error or EOF and returns the data it read. A successful call returns err == nil, not err == io.EOF. It does not treat an EOF from Read as an error to be reported.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: The reader to read all data from

io.Copy.signature: func io.Copy(dst io.Writer, src io.Reader) (written int64, err error)
io.Copy.description: Copies from src to dst until either EOF is reached on src or an error occurs. It returns the number of bytes copied and the first error encountered while copying, if any. A successful Copy returns err == nil, not err == io.EOF.

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: The destination writer

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: The source reader

io.CopyN.signature: func io.CopyN(dst io.Writer, src io.Reader, n int64) (written int64, err error)
io.CopyN.description: Copies n bytes (or until an error) from src to dst. It returns the number of bytes copied and the earliest error encountered while copying. On return, written == n only if err == nil.

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: The destination writer

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: The source reader

parameters.name: n
parameters.description: The number of bytes to copy

io.CopyBuffer.signature: func io.CopyBuffer(dst io.Writer, src io.Reader, buf []byte) (written int64, err error)
io.CopyBuffer.description: Identical to Copy except that it stages through the provided buffer (if one is required) rather than allocating a temporary one. If buf is nil, one is allocated; otherwise if it has zero length, CopyBuffer panics.

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: The destination writer

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: The source reader

parameters.name: buf
parameters.description: An optional buffer to stage the copy through

io.NopCloser.description: Returns a ReadCloser with a no-op Close method wrapping the provided Reader r. If r implements WriterTo, the returned ReadCloser will implement WriterTo by forwarding calls to r.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: The reader to wrap with a no-op closer

io.LimitReader.signature: func io.LimitReader(r io.Reader, n int64) io.Reader
io.LimitReader.description: Returns a Reader that reads from r but stops with EOF after n bytes. The underlying implementation is a *LimitedReader. It is useful for preventing reads beyond a known size limit.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: The underlying reader to limit

parameters.name: n
parameters.description: The maximum number of bytes to read

io.MultiReader.description: Returns a Reader that is the logical concatenation of the provided input readers. They are read sequentially. Once all inputs have returned EOF, Read will return EOF. If any reader returns a non-nil, non-EOF error, Read will return that error.

parameters.name: readers
parameters.description: The readers to concatenate

io.MultiWriter.description: Creates a writer that duplicates its writes to all the provided writers, similar to the Unix tee(1) command. Each write is written to each listed writer one at a time. If a listed writer returns an error, that overall write operation stops and returns the error.

parameters.name: writers
parameters.description: The writers to duplicate output to

io.TeeReader.signature: func io.TeeReader(r io.Reader, w io.Writer) io.Reader
io.TeeReader.description: Returns a Reader that writes to w what it reads from r. All reads from r performed through it are matched with corresponding writes to w. There is no internal buffering-the write must complete before the read completes.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: The source reader

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: The writer to tee data to

io.Pipe.description: Creates a synchronous in-memory pipe. It can be used to connect code expecting an io.Reader with code expecting an io.Writer. Reads and Writes on the pipe are matched one to one; there is no internal buffering.

io.WriteString.signature: func io.WriteString(w io.Writer, s string) (n int, err error)
io.WriteString.description: Writes the contents of the string s to w, which accepts a slice of bytes. If w implements StringWriter, its WriteString method is invoked directly. Otherwise, w.Write is called exactly once with the bytes of s.

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: The writer to write the string to

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The string to write

io.SectionReader.description: Implements Read, Seek, and ReadAt on a section of an underlying ReaderAt. Created with io.NewSectionReader.

io.Discard.description: Discard is a Writer on which all Write calls succeed without doing anything. It is equivalent to /dev/null and is useful when output needs to be discarded but a Writer argument is required.

io.EOF.description: EOF is the error returned by Read when no more input is available. Functions should return EOF only to signal a graceful end of input. If EOF occurs unexpectedly in a structured data stream, the appropriate error is either ErrUnexpectedEOF or some other error giving more detail.

io.ErrUnexpectedEOF.description: ErrUnexpectedEOF means that EOF was encountered in the middle of reading a fixed-size block or data structure. It indicates that the data stream ended prematurely.

io.ErrClosedPipe.description: ErrClosedPipe is the error used for read or write operations on a closed pipe. It is returned when trying to read from a PipeReader after the PipeWriter has been closed, or vice versa.

bufio.NewReader.description: Returns a new Reader whose buffer has the default size (4096 bytes). It wraps an io.Reader providing buffering and some additional reading methods like ReadLine, ReadString, and ReadBytes.

parameters.name: rd
parameters.description: The underlying reader to buffer

bufio.NewReaderSize.description: Returns a new Reader whose buffer has at least the specified size. If the passed reader is already a bufio.Reader with large enough size, it returns the underlying Reader. Otherwise it creates a new one with the given buffer size.

parameters.name: rd
parameters.description: The underlying reader to buffer

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The minimum buffer size in bytes

bufio.NewWriter.description: Returns a new Writer whose buffer has the default size (4096 bytes). If the argument is already a Writer with large enough size, it returns the underlying Writer. Writes are buffered and flushed with Flush.

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: The underlying writer to buffer

bufio.NewWriterSize.description: Returns a new Writer whose buffer has at least the specified size. If the passed writer is already a Writer with large enough size, it returns the underlying Writer. The buffer accumulates writes until Flush is called.

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: The underlying writer to buffer

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The minimum buffer size in bytes

bufio.NewScanner.description: Returns a new Scanner to read from r. The split function defaults to ScanLines. The scanner reads tokens from the input, splitting them using the configured split function. The maximum token size is bufio.MaxScanTokenSize (64 * 1024 bytes).

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: The reader to scan from

bufio.ScanLines.signature: func bufio.ScanLines(data []byte, atEOF bool) (advance int, token []byte, err error)
bufio.ScanLines.description: A split function for a Scanner that returns each line of text, stripping any trailing end-of-line marker. The returned line may be empty. The end-of-line marker is one optional carriage return followed by one mandatory newline.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The input buffer to scan

parameters.name: atEOF
parameters.description: Whether the input has reached EOF

bufio.ScanWords.signature: func bufio.ScanWords(data []byte, atEOF bool) (advance int, token []byte, err error)
bufio.ScanWords.description: A split function for bufio.Scanner that returns each space-separated word of text.

bufio.ScanBytes.signature: func bufio.ScanBytes(data []byte, atEOF bool) (advance int, token []byte, err error)
bufio.ScanBytes.description: A split function for bufio.Scanner that returns each byte as a token.

bufio.ScanRunes.signature: func bufio.ScanRunes(data []byte, atEOF bool) (advance int, token []byte, err error)
bufio.ScanRunes.description: A split function for bufio.Scanner that returns each UTF-8-encoded rune as a token.

bufio.NewReadWriter.description: Allocates a new ReadWriter that dispatches to r and w. It pairs a buffered Reader and a buffered Writer into a single ReadWriter struct that implements both io.Reader and io.Writer.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: The buffered reader

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: The buffered writer

bytes.Buffer.description: Buffer is a variable-sized buffer of bytes with Read and Write methods. The zero value for Buffer is an empty buffer ready to use. It implements io.Reader, io.Writer, io.ByteReader, io.ByteWriter, and io.WriterTo.

bytes.NewBuffer.description: Creates and initializes a new Buffer using buf as its initial contents. The new Buffer takes ownership of buf. In most cases, new(Buffer) or just declaring a Buffer variable is sufficient to initialize a Buffer.

parameters.name: buf
parameters.description: The initial contents of the buffer

bytes.NewBufferString.description: Creates and initializes a new Buffer using the string s as its initial contents. It is intended to prepare a buffer to read an existing string. In most cases, new(Buffer) or just declaring a Buffer variable is sufficient.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The initial string contents of the buffer

bytes.NewReader.description: Returns a new Reader reading from b. Unlike a Buffer, a Reader is read-only and supports seeking. It implements io.Reader, io.ReaderAt, io.Seeker, io.WriterTo, and io.ByteScanner.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: The byte slice to read from

bytes.Contains.description: Reports whether subslice is within b. It performs a byte-by-byte search and returns true if the subslice is found anywhere within the byte slice b.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: The byte slice to search within

parameters.name: subslice
parameters.description: The byte slice to search for

bytes.Equal.description: Reports whether a and b are the same length and contain the same bytes. A nil argument is equivalent to an empty slice. It is a straightforward byte-by-byte comparison.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: The first byte slice to compare

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: The second byte slice to compare

bytes.Compare.description: Returns an integer comparing two byte slices lexicographically. The result is 0 if a == b, -1 if a < b, and +1 if a > b. A nil argument is equivalent to an empty slice.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: The first byte slice to compare

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: The second byte slice to compare

bytes.Replace.signature: func bytes.Replace(s, old, new []byte, n int) []byte
bytes.Replace.description: Returns a copy of the slice s with the first n non-overlapping instances of old replaced by new. If old is empty, it matches at the beginning of the slice and after each UTF-8 sequence. If n < 0, there is no limit on the number of replacements.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The source byte slice

parameters.name: old
parameters.description: The byte pattern to find

parameters.name: new
parameters.description: The replacement bytes

parameters.name: n
parameters.description: Maximum number of replacements; -1 means no limit

bytes.ReplaceAll.description: Returns a copy of the slice s with all non-overlapping instances of old replaced by new. If old is empty, it matches at the beginning of the slice and after each UTF-8 sequence, yielding up to k+1 replacements for a k-byte slice.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The source byte slice

parameters.name: old
parameters.description: The byte pattern to find

parameters.name: new
parameters.description: The replacement bytes

bytes.Split.description: Slices s into all subslices separated by sep and returns a slice of the subslices between those separators. If sep is empty, Split splits after each UTF-8 sequence. The count of items returned is 1 + the number of separators found.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The byte slice to split

parameters.name: sep
parameters.description: The separator to split on

bytes.Join.description: Concatenates the elements of s to create a new byte slice. The separator sep is placed between elements in the resulting slice. It is the inverse of Split.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The slices to join

parameters.name: sep
parameters.description: The separator placed between each element

bytes.TrimSpace.description: Returns a subslice of s by slicing off all leading and trailing white space, as defined by Unicode. It does not allocate new memory; the returned slice shares the same backing array.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The byte slice to trim

bytes.Trim.description: Returns a subslice of s by slicing off all leading and trailing UTF-8-encoded code points contained in cutset. It does not allocate new memory; the returned slice shares the same backing array.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The byte slice to trim

parameters.name: cutset
parameters.description: The set of characters to remove from both ends

bytes.HasPrefix.description: Tests whether the byte slice s begins with prefix. It returns true if s starts with the specified prefix bytes.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The byte slice to check

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: The prefix to look for

bytes.HasSuffix.description: Tests whether the byte slice s ends with suffix. It returns true if s ends with the specified suffix bytes.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The byte slice to check

parameters.name: suffix
parameters.description: The suffix to look for

bytes.Index.description: Returns the index of the first instance of sep in s, or -1 if sep is not present in s. It performs a byte-by-byte search using an efficient algorithm.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The byte slice to search within

parameters.name: sep
parameters.description: The byte pattern to search for

bytes.Count.description: Counts the number of non-overlapping instances of sep in s. If sep is an empty slice, Count returns 1 + the number of UTF-8-encoded code points in s.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The byte slice to search within

parameters.name: sep
parameters.description: The byte pattern to count

bytes.ToLower.description: Returns a copy of the byte slice s with all Unicode letters mapped to their lower case. It allocates a new slice and performs the Unicode case mapping on each character.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The byte slice to convert

bytes.ToUpper.description: Returns a copy of the byte slice s with all Unicode letters mapped to their upper case. It allocates a new slice and performs the Unicode case mapping on each character.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The byte slice to convert

bytes.Fields.description: Splits the byte slice s around each instance of one or more consecutive white space characters, as defined by unicode.IsSpace, returning a slice of subslices of s or an empty slice if s contains only white space.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The byte slice to split into fields

bytes.Repeat.description: Returns a new byte slice consisting of count copies of b. It panics if count is negative or if the result of (len(b) * count) overflows.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: The byte slice to repeat

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: The number of times to repeat

bytes.Map.signature: func bytes.Map(mapping func(r rune) rune, s []byte) []byte
bytes.Map.description: Returns a copy of the byte slice s with all its characters modified according to the mapping function. If mapping returns a negative value, the character is dropped from the byte slice with no replacement.

parameters.name: mapping
parameters.description: The function that maps each rune to its replacement

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The byte slice to apply the mapping to

bytes.Cut.signature: func bytes.Cut(s, sep []byte) (before, after []byte, found bool)
bytes.Cut.description: Slices s around the first instance of sep, returning the text before and after sep. The found result reports whether sep appears in s. If sep does not appear in s, Cut returns s, nil, false.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The byte slice to cut

parameters.name: sep
parameters.description: The separator to cut around

bytes.Clone.description: Returns a copy of b[:len(b)]. The result may have additional unused capacity. Clone(nil) returns nil. It is useful when you need to keep a copy of a byte slice that may be overwritten.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: The byte slice to clone

json.Marshal.description: Returns the JSON encoding of v. It traverses the value v recursively using reflection-based encoding. Struct fields are encoded using the field name or json struct tag. It returns an error if v contains unsupported types like channels, complex numbers, or functions.

parameters.name: v
parameters.description: The Go value to encode as JSON

json.MarshalIndent.signature: func json.MarshalIndent(v any, prefix, indent string) ([]byte, error)
json.MarshalIndent.description: Works like Marshal but applies Indent to format the output. Each element in a JSON object or array begins on a new indented line that starts with prefix followed by one or more copies of indent according to the indentation nesting.

parameters.name: v
parameters.description: The Go value to encode as JSON

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: The string prefix for each line

parameters.name: indent
parameters.description: The string used for each indentation level

json.Unmarshal.description: Parses the JSON-encoded data and stores the result in the value pointed to by v. If v is nil or not a pointer, Unmarshal returns an InvalidUnmarshalError. It uses reflection to map JSON keys to struct fields by name or json tag.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The JSON-encoded byte slice to parse

parameters.name: v
parameters.description: A pointer to the Go value where the result will be stored

json.NewEncoder.description: Returns a new Encoder that writes to w. The encoder writes JSON values to the stream, adding a newline character after each encoded value. It is useful for streaming JSON encoding.

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: The writer to encode JSON to

json.NewDecoder.description: Returns a new Decoder that reads from r. The decoder reads JSON values from the stream one at a time. It is useful for streaming JSON decoding, especially when reading from network connections or files.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: The reader to decode JSON from

json.Valid.description: Reports whether data is a valid JSON encoding. It checks the entire byte slice for valid JSON syntax without decoding or storing the values.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The byte slice to validate as JSON

json.Compact.description: Appends to dst the JSON-encoded src with insignificant space characters elided.

json.Indent.signature: func json.Indent(dst *bytes.Buffer, src []byte, prefix, indent string) error
json.Indent.description: Appends to dst an indented form of the JSON-encoded src.

json.HTMLEscape.description: Appends to dst the JSON-encoded src with <, >, &, U+2028, and U+2029 characters inside string literals replaced by their Unicode escape sequences so that the JSON is safe to embed inside HTML <script> tags.

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: The destination buffer for HTML-escaped JSON

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: The JSON source bytes to escape

json.RawMessage.description: RawMessage is a raw encoded JSON value. It implements Marshaler and Unmarshaler and can be used to delay JSON decoding or precompute a JSON encoding. It allows embedding literal JSON within a larger JSON structure without parsing it.

json.Number.description: Represents a JSON number literal. Use Decoder.UseNumber() to decode numbers as json.Number instead of float64. Methods: Int64(), Float64(), String().

json.Delim.description: Delim is a JSON token delimiter, one of [ ] { }. It is returned by Decoder.Token when the decoder encounters the start or end of a JSON array or object. The Delim type's String method returns the character as a string.

http.ListenAndServe.signature: func http.ListenAndServe(addr string, handler http.Handler) error
http.ListenAndServe.description: Listens on the TCP network address addr and then calls Serve with handler to handle requests on incoming connections. The handler is typically nil, in which case the DefaultServeMux is used. It always returns a non-nil error.

parameters.name: addr
parameters.description: The TCP address to listen on, e.g. ':8080'

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: The handler to invoke; nil means DefaultServeMux

http.ListenAndServeTLS.signature: func http.ListenAndServeTLS(addr, certFile, keyFile string, handler http.Handler) error
http.ListenAndServeTLS.description: Acts identically to ListenAndServe, except that it expects HTTPS connections. The server must be provided with a certificate and matching private key for the TLS configuration. It always returns a non-nil error.

parameters.name: addr
parameters.description: The TCP address to listen on

parameters.name: certFile
parameters.description: Path to the TLS certificate file

parameters.name: keyFile
parameters.description: Path to the TLS private key file

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: The handler to invoke; nil means DefaultServeMux

http.HandleFunc.description: Registers the handler function for the given pattern in the DefaultServeMux. The pattern can be a fixed path like '/favicon.ico' or a subtree like '/images/'. It is a convenience wrapper around Handle.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: The URL pattern to match

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: The handler function to call for matching requests

http.Handle.description: Registers the handler for the given pattern in the DefaultServeMux. If a handler already exists for pattern, Handle panics. The pattern defines which URLs are routed to this handler.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: The URL pattern to match

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: The handler to register

http.Get.description: Issues a GET to the specified URL using the DefaultClient. When err is nil, resp always contains a non-nil resp.Body. Caller should close resp.Body when done reading from it. It follows redirects, up to a maximum of 10.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to send the GET request to

http.Post.signature: func http.Post(url, contentType string, body io.Reader) (resp *http.Response, err error)
http.Post.description: Issues a POST to the specified URL using the DefaultClient. Caller should close resp.Body when done reading from it. If the provided body is an io.Closer, it is closed after the request.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to send the POST request to

parameters.name: contentType
parameters.description: The Content-Type of the body

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The request body to send

http.PostForm.signature: func http.PostForm(url string, data url.Values) (resp *http.Response, err error)
http.PostForm.description: Issues a POST to the specified URL with data's keys and values URL-encoded as the request body. The Content-Type header is set to application/x-www-form-urlencoded. Caller should close resp.Body when done reading from it.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to send the POST request to

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The form data to encode and send

http.Head.description: Issues a HEAD to the specified URL using the DefaultClient. It is like Get but only retrieves the headers, not the body. Caller should close resp.Body when done reading from it.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to send the HEAD request to

http.NewRequest.signature: func http.NewRequest(method, url string, body io.Reader) (*http.Request, error)
http.NewRequest.description: Returns a new Request given a method, URL, and optional body. If the provided body is also an io.Closer, the returned Request.Body is set to body and will be closed by the Client methods Do, Post, and PostForm. NewRequest returns a Request suitable for use with Client.Do.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: The HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, etc.)

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL for the request

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The request body; nil means no body

http.NewRequestWithContext.signature: func http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx context.Context, method, url string, body io.Reader) (*http.Request, error)
http.NewRequestWithContext.description: Returns a new Request given a method, URL, optional body, and context. If the provided body is also an io.Closer, the returned Request.Body is set to body. The context controls the entire lifetime of a request and its response.

parameters.name: ctx
parameters.description: The context for the request lifetime

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: The HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, etc.)

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL for the request

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The request body; nil means no body

http.NewServeMux.description: Allocates and returns a new ServeMux.

http.Error.signature: func http.Error(w http.ResponseWriter, error string, code int)
http.Error.description: Replies to the request with the specified error message and HTTP code. It sets the Content-Type header to text/plain and writes the error string as the response body. The error message should be plain text.

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: The response writer

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: The error message to send

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: The HTTP status code

http.NotFound.description: Replies to the request with an HTTP 404 not found error. It is a convenience handler that writes a standard '404 page not found' response.

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: The response writer

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: The incoming request

http.Redirect.signature: func http.Redirect(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, url string, code int)
http.Redirect.description: Replies to the request with a redirect to url, which may be a path relative to the request path. The provided code should be in the 3xx range and is usually StatusMovedPermanently (301), StatusFound (302), or StatusSeeOther (303).

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: The response writer

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: The incoming request

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The redirect target URL

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: The HTTP redirect status code
