http.ServeFile.signature: func http.ServeFile(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, name string)
http.ServeFile.description: Replies to the request with the contents of the named file or directory. If the provided file or directory name is a relative path, it is interpreted relative to the current directory. ServeFile handles If-Modified-Since and range requests.

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: The response writer

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: The incoming request

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The path to the file or directory to serve

http.FileServer.description: Returns a handler that serves HTTP requests with the contents of the file system rooted at root. It handles requests for directory listings and individual files. Use http.Dir to convert an OS directory path to an http.FileSystem.

parameters.name: root
parameters.description: The root of the file system to serve

http.StripPrefix.signature: func http.StripPrefix(prefix string, h http.Handler) http.Handler
http.StripPrefix.description: Returns a handler that serves HTTP requests by removing the given prefix from the request URL's path and invoking the handler h. It replies with an HTTP 404 not found error if the request path doesn't start with the prefix.

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: The URL path prefix to strip

parameters.name: h
parameters.description: The handler to invoke after stripping the prefix

http.MaxBytesReader.signature: func http.MaxBytesReader(w http.ResponseWriter, r io.ReadCloser, n int64) io.ReadCloser
http.MaxBytesReader.description: Similar to io.LimitReader but limits the body of an incoming request. When the limit is exceeded, returns an error and sends 413 Request Entity Too Large.

http.SetCookie.description: Adds a Set-Cookie header to the provided ResponseWriter's headers.

http.StatusText.description: Returns a text for the HTTP status code. It returns the empty string if the code is unknown. For example, StatusText(200) returns 'OK' and StatusText(404) returns 'Not Found'.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: The HTTP status code

http.CanonicalHeaderKey.description: Returns the canonical format of the header key s. The canonicalization converts the first letter and any letter following a hyphen to upper case; the rest are converted to lower case. For example, 'accept-encoding' becomes 'Accept-Encoding'.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: The header key to canonicalize

http.DetectContentType.description: Implements the algorithm described at https://mimesniff.spec.whatwg.org/ to determine the Content-Type of the given data. It considers at most the first 512 bytes of data. It always returns a valid MIME type; if it cannot determine a more specific one, it returns 'application/octet-stream'.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The data to detect the content type of

http.Handler.description: Handler responds to an HTTP request. ServeHTTP should write reply headers and data to the ResponseWriter and then return. It is the fundamental interface for HTTP request handling in Go's net/http package.

http.HandlerFunc.description: HandlerFunc is an adapter to allow the use of ordinary functions as HTTP handlers. If f is a function with the appropriate signature, HandlerFunc(f) is a Handler that calls f. It is the simplest way to create an HTTP handler.

http.ResponseWriter.signature: type http.ResponseWriter interface { Header() Header; Write([]byte) (int, error); WriteHeader(statusCode int) }
http.ResponseWriter.description: ResponseWriter is used by an HTTP handler to construct an HTTP response. A ResponseWriter may not be used after the Handler.ServeHTTP method has returned. It provides methods to set headers, write body data, and set the status code.

http.Server.signature: type http.Server struct { Addr string; Handler Handler; TLSConfig *tls.Config; ReadTimeout time.Duration; WriteTimeout time.Duration; IdleTimeout time.Duration; MaxHeaderBytes int; ... }
http.Server.description: Defines parameters for running an HTTP server. Use ListenAndServe or Serve methods for more control than the top-level functions.

http.Client.signature: type http.Client struct { Transport RoundTripper; CheckRedirect func(req *Request, via []*Request) error; Jar CookieJar; Timeout time.Duration }
http.Client.description: Client is an HTTP client. Its zero value (DefaultClient) is a usable client that uses DefaultTransport. The Client's Transport typically has internal state (cached TCP connections), so Clients should be reused instead of created as needed.

http.Transport.description: Transport is an implementation of RoundTripper that supports HTTP, HTTPS, and HTTP proxies. By default, Transport caches connections for future re-use. It is safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines and should be reused rather than created as needed.

http.Cookie.signature: type http.Cookie struct { Name string; Value string; Path string; Domain string; Expires time.Time; MaxAge int; Secure bool; HttpOnly bool; SameSite SameSite }
http.Cookie.description: Cookie represents an HTTP cookie as sent in the Set-Cookie header of an HTTP response or the Cookie header of an HTTP request. It contains fields for name, value, expiration, security settings, and scope restrictions.

http.Header.description: Represents the key-value pairs in an HTTP header. Keys are canonicalized (Title-Case). Methods: Get, Set, Add, Del, Clone, Values.

http.Request.signature: type http.Request struct { Method string; URL *url.URL; Header Header; Body io.ReadCloser; ... }
http.Request.description: Request represents an HTTP request received by a server or to be sent by a client. The field semantics differ slightly between client and server usage. For server handlers, the Body is always non-nil but will return EOF immediately when no body is present.

http.Response.signature: type http.Response struct { Status string; StatusCode int; Header Header; Body io.ReadCloser; ... }
http.Response.description: Response represents the response from an HTTP request. The Client returns Responses from calls to Client.Do, Client.Get, etc. The Body field is always non-nil and the caller must close it when finished reading. It contains the server's reply headers and body.

context.Background.description: Returns a non-nil, empty Context. It is never canceled, has no values, and has no deadline. It is typically used by the main function, initialization, and tests, and as the top-level Context for incoming requests.

context.TODO.description: Returns a non-nil, empty Context. Code should use context.TODO when it's unclear which Context to use or it is not yet available (because the surrounding function has not yet been extended to accept a Context parameter).

context.WithCancel.signature: func context.WithCancel(parent Context) (ctx Context, cancel CancelFunc)
context.WithCancel.description: Returns a copy of parent with a new Done channel. The returned context's Done channel is closed when the returned cancel function is called or when the parent context's Done channel is closed, whichever happens first. Canceling this context releases resources associated with it.

context.WithCancelCause.signature: func context.WithCancelCause(parent Context) (ctx Context, cancel CancelCauseFunc)
context.WithCancelCause.description: Like WithCancel but the cancel function takes an error (cause). If canceled with a non-nil error, that error is retrievable via context.Cause(ctx). Added in Go 1.20.

context.WithTimeout.signature: func context.WithTimeout(parent Context, timeout time.Duration) (Context, CancelFunc)
context.WithTimeout.description: Returns WithDeadline(parent, time.Now().Add(timeout)). The returned context is done when the timeout elapses, the returned cancel function is called, or the parent's Done channel is closed. Always call cancel as soon as operations are done.

context.WithDeadline.signature: func context.WithDeadline(parent Context, d time.Time) (Context, CancelFunc)
context.WithDeadline.description: Returns a copy of the parent context with the deadline adjusted to be no later than d. If the parent's deadline is already earlier than d, WithDeadline(parent, d) is semantically equivalent to parent. Always defer cancel().

context.WithValue.signature: func context.WithValue(parent Context, key, val any) Context
context.WithValue.description: Returns a copy of parent in which the value associated with key is val. Use context Values only for request-scoped data that transits processes and APIs, not for passing optional parameters to functions. The key should be unexported to avoid collisions.

context.WithoutCancel.description: Returns a copy of parent that is not canceled when parent is canceled. The returned context returns no Deadline or Err, and its Done channel is nil. Calling Cause on the returned context returns nil. Added in Go 1.21.

context.AfterFunc.signature: func context.AfterFunc(ctx Context, f func()) (stop func() bool)
context.AfterFunc.description: Arranges to call f in its own goroutine after ctx is done (canceled or timed out). Returns a stop function that prevents f from being run. Added in Go 1.21.

context.Cause.description: Returns a non-nil error explaining why a context was canceled. The first call to CancelCauseFunc(err) sets the cause. If ctx was canceled via WithCancel, Cause returns the same as ctx.Err(). Added in Go 1.20.

context.Context.signature: type context.Context interface { Deadline() (time.Time, bool); Done() <-chan struct{}; Err() error; Value(key any) any }
context.Context.description: Context carries deadlines, cancellation signals, and request-scoped values across API boundaries and between processes. Server programs should propagate Contexts along the call chain. Methods on Context may be called by multiple goroutines simultaneously.

sync.Mutex.description: A Mutex is a mutual exclusion lock. The zero value for a Mutex is an unlocked mutex. A Mutex must not be copied after first use. Use Lock()/Unlock() to acquire/release. Prefer defer mu.Unlock() immediately after Lock().

sync.Mutex.Lock.description: Locks the mutex. If the lock is already in use, the calling goroutine blocks until the mutex is available. Always pair with defer mu.Unlock().

sync.Mutex.Unlock.description: Unlocks the mutex. A runtime error occurs if the mutex is not locked on entry to Unlock. A locked Mutex is not associated with a particular goroutine - it is allowed for one goroutine to lock and another to unlock it.

sync.Mutex.TryLock.description: Tries to lock the mutex and reports whether it succeeded. TryLock does not block. Note: use of TryLock is often a sign of a broken design; prefer a proper synchronization pattern.

sync.RWMutex.description: A RWMutex is a reader/writer mutual exclusion lock. The lock can be held by an arbitrary number of readers or a single writer. The zero value is an unlocked mutex. A RWMutex must not be copied after first use.

sync.RWMutex.RLock.description: Locks rw for reading. Multiple goroutines can simultaneously hold RLock. It should not be used for recursive read locking; a blocked Lock call excludes new readers.

sync.RWMutex.RUnlock.description: Undoes a single RLock call. A runtime error occurs if rw is not locked for reading.

sync.WaitGroup.description: A WaitGroup waits for a collection of goroutines to finish. The main goroutine calls Add to set the number to wait for. Then each goroutine runs and calls Done when finished. Wait blocks until all goroutines have finished.

sync.WaitGroup.Add.description: Adds delta (may be negative) to the WaitGroup counter. If the counter becomes zero, all goroutines blocked on Wait are released. If the counter goes negative, a panic occurs. Calls with positive delta must happen before Wait.

sync.WaitGroup.Done.description: Decrements the WaitGroup counter by one. Equivalent to Add(-1). Typically deferred: defer wg.Done().

sync.WaitGroup.Wait.description: Blocks until the WaitGroup counter is zero. Used in the main goroutine to wait for all spawned goroutines to complete.

sync.Once.description: Once is an object that will perform exactly one action. A Once must not be copied after first use. Commonly used for lazy initialization or singleton patterns.

sync.Once.Do.description: Calls the function f if and only if Do is being called for the first time for this instance of Once. If once.Do(f) is called multiple times, only the first call will invoke f, even if f has a different value in each invocation.

sync.OnceFunc.description: Returns a function that invokes f only once. The returned function may be called concurrently. If f panics, the returned function will panic with the same value on every call. Added in Go 1.21.

sync.OnceValue.signature: func sync.OnceValue[T any](f func() T) func() T
sync.OnceValue.description: Returns a function that invokes f only once and returns the value returned by f. Added in Go 1.21.

sync.OnceValues.signature: func sync.OnceValues[T1, T2 any](f func() (T1, T2)) func() (T1, T2)
sync.OnceValues.description: Returns a function that invokes f only once and returns the two values returned by f. Added in Go 1.21.

sync.Map.description: Map is like a Go map[any]any but is safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines without additional locking. The Map type is specialized for two use cases: (1) when entries are only written once but read many times, (2) when multiple goroutines read/write disjoint key sets.

sync.Map.Load.signature: func (*sync.Map) Load(key any) (value any, ok bool)
sync.Map.Load.description: Returns the value stored in the map for a key, or nil if no value is present. The ok result indicates whether value was found.

sync.Map.Store.description: Sets the value for a key.

sync.Map.LoadOrStore.signature: func (*sync.Map) LoadOrStore(key, value any) (actual any, loaded bool)
sync.Map.LoadOrStore.description: Returns the existing value for the key if present. Otherwise, it stores and returns the given value. The loaded result is true if the value was loaded, false if stored.

sync.Map.LoadAndDelete.signature: func (*sync.Map) LoadAndDelete(key any) (value any, loaded bool)
sync.Map.LoadAndDelete.description: Deletes the value for a key, returning the previous value if any. The loaded result reports whether the key was present.

sync.Map.Delete.description: Deletes the value for a key.

sync.Map.Range.signature: func (*sync.Map) Range(f func(key, value any) bool)
sync.Map.Range.description: Calls f sequentially for each key and value present in the map. If f returns false, range stops the iteration.

sync.Pool.signature: type sync.Pool struct { New func() any }
sync.Pool.description: A Pool is a set of temporary objects that may be individually saved and retrieved. Items in the Pool may be removed automatically at any time without notification. A Pool is safe for use by multiple goroutines simultaneously. Pool's purpose is to cache allocated but unused items for later reuse.

sync.Pool.Get.description: Selects an arbitrary item from the Pool, removes it from the Pool, and returns it to the caller. Get may choose to ignore the pool and treat it as empty. Callers should not assume any relation between values passed to Put and Get.

sync.Pool.Put.description: Adds x to the pool. The pool may discard the item at any time.

sync.Cond.description: Cond implements a condition variable, a rendezvous point for goroutines waiting for or announcing the occurrence of an event. Each Cond has an associated Locker L (often a *Mutex or *RWMutex). Create with sync.NewCond(&mu).

sync.NewCond.description: Returns a new Cond with Locker l. Typically pass a *sync.Mutex or *sync.RWMutex.

sync.Cond.Wait.description: Atomically unlocks c.L and suspends the calling goroutine. After later resuming execution, Wait locks c.L before returning. Must be called inside a for loop checking the condition: for !condition { c.Wait() }.

sync.Cond.Signal.description: Wakes one goroutine waiting on c, if there is any. The caller does not need to hold c.L during the call. Signal() does not affect goroutine scheduling priority.

sync.Cond.Broadcast.description: Wakes all goroutines waiting on c. The caller does not need to hold c.L during the call.

atomic.AddInt64.description: Atomically adds delta to *addr and returns the new value.

atomic.LoadInt64.description: Atomically loads and returns the value stored in *addr.

atomic.CompareAndSwapInt64.signature: func atomic.CompareAndSwapInt64(addr *int64, old, new int64) (swapped bool)
atomic.CompareAndSwapInt64.description: Executes the compare-and-swap operation for an int64 value. Atomically: if *addr == old, set *addr = new and return true. Otherwise return false.

atomic.SwapInt64.description: Atomically stores new into *addr and returns the previous *addr value.

atomic.Bool.description: An atomic boolean value. The zero value is false. A Bool must not be copied after first use. Added in Go 1.19. Methods: Load, Store, Swap, CompareAndSwap.

atomic.Int32.description: An atomic int32 value. The zero value is zero. An Int32 must not be copied after first use. Added in Go 1.19. Methods: Load, Store, Add, Swap, CompareAndSwap.

atomic.Int64.description: An atomic int64 value. The zero value is zero. An Int64 must not be copied after first use. Added in Go 1.19. Methods: Load, Store, Add, Swap, CompareAndSwap.

atomic.Uint32.description: An atomic uint32 value. Added in Go 1.19. Methods: Load, Store, Add, Swap, CompareAndSwap.

atomic.Uint64.description: An atomic uint64 value. Added in Go 1.19. Methods: Load, Store, Add, Swap, CompareAndSwap.

atomic.Pointer.description: An atomic pointer of type *T. Added in Go 1.19. Methods: Load() *T, Store(*T), Swap(*T) *T, CompareAndSwap(old, new *T) bool. Replaces unsafe.Pointer atomics.

atomic.Value.description: Provides an atomic load and store of a consistently typed value. The zero value for a Value returns nil from Load. Once Store is called, a Value must not be copied. A Value must not be stored as a different type than the first Store call.

time.Since.description: Returns the time elapsed since t. Shorthand for time.Now().Sub(t).

time.Until.description: Returns the duration until t. Shorthand for t.Sub(time.Now()).

time.Sleep.description: Pauses the current goroutine for at least the duration d. A negative or zero duration causes Sleep to return immediately.

time.After.description: Waits for the duration to elapse and then sends the current time on the returned channel. Equivalent to NewTimer(d).C. The underlying Timer is not recovered by GC until it fires; use NewTimer + Stop for efficiency.

time.Tick.description: Convenience wrapper for NewTicker providing access only to its channel. It leaks the ticker; use NewTicker instead if you need to shut it down.

time.NewTicker.description: Returns a new Ticker containing a channel that will send the current time with a period specified by the duration argument d. It adjusts intervals or drops ticks to make up for slow receivers. d must be > 0. Stop the ticker to release resources.

time.NewTimer.description: Creates a new Timer that will send the current time on its channel after at least duration d. Use timer.Stop() to cancel and timer.Reset(d) to reuse.

time.AfterFunc.description: Waits for the duration to elapse and then calls f in its own goroutine. Returns a Timer that can be used to cancel the call using Stop.

time.Parse.description: Parses a formatted string and returns the time value it represents. The layout defines the format by showing how the reference time, Mon Jan 2 15:04:05 -0700 MST 2006, would be represented in the format. Common layouts: time.RFC3339, time.DateTime.

time.ParseDuration.description: Parses a duration string. Valid time units are 'ns', 'us' (or 'µs'), 'ms', 's', 'm', 'h'. Example: '300ms', '1.5h', '2h45m'.

time.Date.signature: func time.Date(year int, month time.Month, day, hour, min, sec, nsec int, loc *time.Location) time.Time
time.Date.description: Returns the Time corresponding to yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss + nsec nanoseconds in the appropriate zone for that time in the given location.

time.LoadLocation.description: Returns the Location with the given name, e.g. 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London'. 'UTC' and 'Local' are always recognized.

time.Time.description: Represents an instant in time with nanosecond precision. The zero value is January 1, year 1, 00:00:00 UTC. Programs using times should typically store and pass them as values, not pointers. A Time value can be used by multiple goroutines simultaneously.

time.Time.Format.description: Returns a textual representation of the time value formatted according to the layout defined by the argument. The layout uses the reference time 'Mon Jan 2 15:04:05 MST 2006'. Common: time.RFC3339, time.DateTime, time.DateOnly.

time.Time.Sub.description: Returns the duration t-u. If the result exceeds the maximum (or minimum) value, the maximum (or minimum) duration will be returned.

time.Time.Before.description: Reports whether the time instant t is before u.

time.Time.After.description: Reports whether the time instant t is after u.

time.Time.Equal.description: Reports whether t and u represent the same time instant. Two times can be equal even if they are in different locations. Use == only with Time values from the same location.

time.Time.IsZero.description: Reports whether t represents the zero time instant: January 1, year 1, 00:00:00 UTC.

time.Time.Unix.description: Returns t as a Unix time, the number of seconds elapsed since January 1, 1970 UTC.

time.Time.UnixMilli.description: Returns t as a Unix time, the number of milliseconds elapsed since January 1, 1970 UTC.

time.Time.UTC.description: Returns t with the location set to UTC.

time.Time.Local.description: Returns t with the location set to local time.

time.Time.In.description: Returns a copy of t representing the same time instant, but associated with a given location for display purposes.

time.Time.Truncate.description: Returns the result of rounding t down to a multiple of d. If d <= 0, returns t stripped of any monotonic clock reading.

time.Time.Round.description: Returns the result of rounding t to the nearest multiple of d.

time.Duration.description: Represents the elapsed time between two instants as an int64 nanosecond count. The largest representable duration is approximately 290 years. Constants: time.Nanosecond, Microsecond, Millisecond, Second, Minute, Hour.

time.Duration.String.description: Returns a string representing the duration in the form '72h3m0.5s'. Leading zero units are omitted.

time.Duration.Seconds.description: Returns the duration as a floating point number of seconds.

time.Duration.Milliseconds.description: Returns the duration as an integer millisecond count.

sort.Ints.description: Sorts a slice of ints in increasing order. Deprecated in Go 1.21+: use slices.Sort instead.

sort.Strings.description: Sorts a slice of strings in increasing order. Deprecated in Go 1.21+: use slices.Sort instead.

sort.Float64s.description: Sorts a slice of float64s in increasing order. Not-a-number values are ordered before other values.

sort.Slice.signature: func sort.Slice(x any, less func(i, j int) bool)
sort.Slice.description: Sorts the slice x given the provided less function. The sort is not guaranteed to be stable. The less function reports whether x[i] should come before x[j].

sort.SliceStable.signature: func sort.SliceStable(x any, less func(i, j int) bool)
sort.SliceStable.description: Sorts the slice x using the provided less function, keeping the original order of equal elements.

sort.SliceIsSorted.signature: func sort.SliceIsSorted(x any, less func(i, j int) bool) bool
sort.SliceIsSorted.description: Reports whether the slice x is sorted according to the provided less function.

sort.Search.signature: func sort.Search(n int, f func(int) bool) int
sort.Search.description: Uses binary search to find the smallest index i in [0, n) at which f(i) is true, assuming f(i) == true implies f(i+1) == true.

sort.SearchInts.description: Searches for x in a sorted slice of ints and returns the index as specified by Search. The slice must be sorted.

sort.SearchStrings.description: Searches for x in a sorted slice of strings and returns the index as specified by Search.

sort.Reverse.description: Returns a sort.Interface that sorts in reverse order. Use with sort.Sort(): sort.Sort(sort.Reverse(sort.IntSlice(s))).

sort.Interface.signature: type sort.Interface interface { Len() int; Less(i, j int) bool; Swap(i, j int) }
sort.Interface.description: Sort interface for custom sorting. Any type implementing Len, Less, and Swap can be sorted with sort.Sort.

slices.Sort.description: Sorts a slice of any ordered type in ascending order. Uses pattern-defeating quicksort. Added in Go 1.21.

slices.SortFunc.signature: func slices.SortFunc[S ~[]E, E any](x S, cmp func(a, b E) int)
slices.SortFunc.description: Sorts the slice x in ascending order as determined by the cmp function. cmp(a, b) should return negative if a < b, 0 if equal, positive if a > b.

slices.SortStableFunc.signature: func slices.SortStableFunc[S ~[]E, E any](x S, cmp func(a, b E) int)
slices.SortStableFunc.description: Sorts the slice x while keeping the original order of equal elements, as determined by cmp.

slices.BinarySearch.signature: func slices.BinarySearch[S ~[]E, E cmp.Ordered](x S, target E) (int, bool)
slices.BinarySearch.description: Searches for target in a sorted slice and returns the position where target is found, or the position where it would be inserted. The bool reports whether an exact match was found.

slices.BinarySearchFunc.signature: func slices.BinarySearchFunc[S ~[]E, E, T any](x S, target T, cmp func(E, T) int) (int, bool)
slices.BinarySearchFunc.description: Binary search with a custom comparison function.

slices.Contains.signature: func slices.Contains[S ~[]E, E comparable](s S, v E) bool
slices.Contains.description: Reports whether v is present in s.

slices.ContainsFunc.signature: func slices.ContainsFunc[S ~[]E, E any](s S, f func(E) bool) bool
slices.ContainsFunc.description: Reports whether at least one element in s satisfies f.

slices.Index.signature: func slices.Index[S ~[]E, E comparable](s S, v E) int
slices.Index.description: Returns the index of the first occurrence of v in s, or -1 if not present.

slices.IndexFunc.signature: func slices.IndexFunc[S ~[]E, E any](s S, f func(E) bool) int
slices.IndexFunc.description: Returns the first index i satisfying f(s[i]), or -1 if none do.

slices.Min.signature: func slices.Min[S ~[]E, E cmp.Ordered](x S) E
slices.Min.description: Returns the minimal value in x. Panics if x is empty.

slices.Max.signature: func slices.Max[S ~[]E, E cmp.Ordered](x S) E
slices.Max.description: Returns the maximal value in x. Panics if x is empty.

slices.Reverse.description: Reverses the elements of the slice in place.

slices.Compact.signature: func slices.Compact[S ~[]E, E comparable](s S) S
slices.Compact.description: Replaces consecutive runs of equal elements with a single copy (like Unix uniq). Modifies the slice in place and returns the modified slice.

slices.CompactFunc.signature: func slices.CompactFunc[S ~[]E, E any](s S, eq func(E, E) bool) S
slices.CompactFunc.description: Like Compact but uses a custom equality function.

slices.Clip.signature: func slices.Clip[S ~[]E, E any](s S) S
slices.Clip.description: Removes unused capacity from the slice, returning s[:len(s):len(s)].

slices.Clone.signature: func slices.Clone[S ~[]E, E any](s S) S
slices.Clone.description: Returns a copy of the slice. Elements are copied using assignment, so this is a shallow clone.

slices.Grow.signature: func slices.Grow[S ~[]E, E any](s S, n int) S
slices.Grow.description: Increases the slice's capacity, if necessary, to guarantee space for another n elements. After Grow(n), at least n elements can be appended without another allocation.

slices.Delete.signature: func slices.Delete[S ~[]E, E any](s S, i, j int) S
slices.Delete.description: Removes the elements s[i:j] from s, returning the modified slice. Panics if i > j or the indices are out of range.

slices.DeleteFunc.signature: func slices.DeleteFunc[S ~[]E, E any](s S, del func(E) bool) S
slices.DeleteFunc.description: Removes any elements from s for which del returns true, returning the modified slice.

slices.Replace.signature: func slices.Replace[S ~[]E, E any](s S, i, j int, v ...E) S
slices.Replace.description: Replaces the elements s[i:j] by the given values v..., returning the modified slice.

slices.Insert.signature: func slices.Insert[S ~[]E, E any](s S, i int, v ...E) S
slices.Insert.description: Inserts the values v... into s at index i, returning the modified slice.

slices.Concat.description: Returns a new slice concatenating the passed in slices. Added in Go 1.22.

slices.Equal.signature: func slices.Equal[S ~[]E, E comparable](s1, s2 S) bool
slices.Equal.description: Reports whether two slices are equal: the same length and all elements equal. If lengths differ, Equal returns false.

slices.EqualFunc.signature: func slices.EqualFunc[S1 ~[]E1, S2 ~[]E2, E1, E2 any](s1 S1, s2 S2, eq func(E1, E2) bool) bool
slices.EqualFunc.description: Reports whether two slices are equal using a custom equality function for elements.

slices.Compare.signature: func slices.Compare[S ~[]E, E cmp.Ordered](s1, s2 S) int
slices.Compare.description: Compares two slices element by element using < and >. Returns -1, 0, or +1.

slices.IsSorted.signature: func slices.IsSorted[S ~[]E, E cmp.Ordered](x S) bool
slices.IsSorted.description: Reports whether x is sorted in ascending order.

slices.IsSortedFunc.signature: func slices.IsSortedFunc[S ~[]E, E any](x S, cmp func(a, b E) int) bool
slices.IsSortedFunc.description: Reports whether x is sorted in ascending order, with comparison defined by cmp.

maps.Keys.signature: func maps.Keys[M ~map[K]V, K comparable, V any](m M) []K
maps.Keys.description: Returns the keys of the map m in an indeterminate order.

maps.Values.signature: func maps.Values[M ~map[K]V, K comparable, V any](m M) []V
maps.Values.description: Returns the values of the map m in an indeterminate order.

maps.Clone.signature: func maps.Clone[M ~map[K]V, K comparable, V any](m M) M
maps.Clone.description: Returns a shallow copy of the map m.

maps.Copy.signature: func maps.Copy[M1 ~map[K]V, M2 ~map[K]V, K comparable, V any](dst M1, src M2)
maps.Copy.description: Copies all key/value pairs from src into dst. When a key in src is already present in dst, the value in dst will be overwritten.

maps.DeleteFunc.signature: func maps.DeleteFunc[M ~map[K]V, K comparable, V any](m M, del func(K, V) bool)
maps.DeleteFunc.description: Deletes any key/value pairs from m for which del returns true.

maps.Equal.signature: func maps.Equal[M1, M2 ~map[K]V, K, V comparable](m1 M1, m2 M2) bool
maps.Equal.description: Reports whether two maps contain the same key/value pairs.

maps.EqualFunc.signature: func maps.EqualFunc[M1 ~map[K]V1, M2 ~map[K]V2, K comparable, V1, V2 any](m1 M1, m2 M2, eq func(V1, V2) bool) bool
maps.EqualFunc.description: Reports whether two maps contain the same keys with values satisfying eq.

regexp.Compile.description: Parses a regular expression and returns, if successful, a Regexp object that can be used to match against text. For constant patterns, use MustCompile.

regexp.MustCompile.description: Like Compile but panics if the expression cannot be parsed. It simplifies safe initialization of global variables holding compiled regular expressions.

regexp.MatchString.signature: func regexp.MatchString(pattern string, s string) (matched bool, err error)
regexp.MatchString.description: Reports whether the string s contains any match of the regular expression pattern. For repeated use, compile the pattern first.

regexp.Regexp.MatchString.description: Reports whether the string s contains any match of the regular expression re.

regexp.Regexp.FindString.description: Returns a string holding the text of the leftmost match in s. If there is no match, returns the empty string.

regexp.Regexp.FindStringIndex.description: Returns a two-element slice of integers [start, end] defining the location of the leftmost match.

regexp.Regexp.FindAllString.description: Returns a slice of all successive matches of the expression. A return of nil indicates no match. If n >= 0, returns at most n matches.

regexp.Regexp.FindStringSubmatch.description: Returns a slice holding the text of the leftmost match plus submatches (capturing groups). Element 0 is the full match, 1 is first group, etc.

regexp.Regexp.FindAllStringSubmatch.description: Returns a slice of all successive matches plus submatches.

regexp.Regexp.ReplaceAllString.description: Returns a copy of src, replacing matches of the Regexp with the replacement repl. Inside repl, $ signs are interpreted as in Expand.

regexp.Regexp.ReplaceAllStringFunc.signature: func (*regexp.Regexp) ReplaceAllStringFunc(src string, repl func(string) string) string
regexp.Regexp.ReplaceAllStringFunc.description: Returns a copy of src replacing matches with the return value of the function repl applied to the matched substring.

regexp.Regexp.Split.description: Slices s into substrings separated by the expression and returns a slice of the substrings. If n > 0, returns at most n substrings.

regexp.Regexp.SubexpNames.description: Returns the names of the parenthesized subexpressions. The first element is '' (the whole match has no name). Named groups use (?P<name>...).

regexp.QuoteMeta.description: Returns a string that escapes all regular expression metacharacters inside the argument text; the returned string is a regular expression matching the literal text.

filepath.Join.description: Joins any number of path elements into a single path, separating them with an OS specific separator. Empty elements are ignored. The result is Cleaned. On Windows, uses backslash.

filepath.Dir.description: Returns all but the last element of path, typically the path's directory. Similar to Unix dirname.

filepath.Base.description: Returns the last element of path. Trailing path separators are removed. If empty, returns '.'. Similar to Unix basename.

filepath.Ext.description: Returns the file name extension including the '.'. Returns '' if there is no dot.

filepath.Abs.description: Returns an absolute representation of path. If not absolute, joins with current working directory.

filepath.Rel.description: Returns a relative path that is lexically equivalent to targpath when joined to basepath with an intervening separator.

filepath.Clean.description: Returns the shortest path name equivalent to path by purely lexical processing (resolving . and .., eliminating double slashes, etc).

filepath.Split.description: Splits path immediately following the final separator, separating it into a directory and file name. If no separator, dir is empty and file is path.

filepath.Walk.signature: func filepath.Walk(root string, fn filepath.WalkFunc) error
filepath.Walk.description: Walks the file tree rooted at root, calling fn for each file or directory in the tree. Deprecated: use WalkDir for better performance (does not call os.Lstat on every visited file).

filepath.WalkDir.signature: func filepath.WalkDir(root string, fn fs.WalkDirFunc) error
filepath.WalkDir.description: Walks the file tree rooted at root, calling fn for each file or directory. More efficient than Walk (added Go 1.16). Uses fs.DirEntry instead of os.FileInfo.

filepath.Glob.description: Returns the names of all files matching pattern, which can contain *, ?, and [...] but not **.

filepath.Match.signature: func filepath.Match(pattern, name string) (matched bool, err error)
filepath.Match.description: Reports whether name matches the shell file name pattern. Supports wildcards: * (any sequence), ? (single char), [lo-hi] (range).

filepath.IsAbs.description: Reports whether the path is absolute.

filepath.EvalSymlinks.description: Returns the path name after evaluation of any symbolic links. Resolves both relative and absolute symlinks.

filepath.FromSlash.description: Returns the result of replacing each '/' in path with the OS separator.

filepath.ToSlash.description: Returns the result of replacing each OS separator in path with '/'.

log.Println.description: Calls Output to print to the standard logger with a newline. Arguments are handled in the manner of fmt.Println.

log.Printf.description: Calls Output to print to the standard logger. Arguments are handled in the manner of fmt.Printf.

log.Fatal.description: Equivalent to Print() followed by a call to os.Exit(1).

log.Fatalf.description: Equivalent to Printf() followed by a call to os.Exit(1).

log.Panic.description: Equivalent to Print() followed by a call to panic().

log.Panicf.description: Equivalent to Printf() followed by a call to panic().

log.SetFlags.description: Sets the output flags for the standard logger. Constants: Ldate, Ltime, Lmicroseconds, Llongfile, Lshortfile, LUTC, Lmsgprefix, LstdFlags.

log.SetPrefix.description: Sets the output prefix for the standard logger.

log.SetOutput.description: Sets the output destination for the standard logger.

log.New.signature: func log.New(out io.Writer, prefix string, flag int) *log.Logger
log.New.description: Creates a new Logger. The out variable sets the destination for log data. The prefix appears at the beginning of each log line. The flag argument defines the logging properties.

slog.Info.description: Calls Logger.Info on the default logger. Structured logging with key-value pairs: slog.Info("msg", "key", value).

slog.Warn.description: Calls Logger.Warn on the default logger.

slog.Error.description: Calls Logger.Error on the default logger.

slog.Debug.description: Calls Logger.Debug on the default logger.

slog.With.description: Calls Logger.With on the default logger, returning a new Logger that includes the given attributes in each output.

slog.New.description: Creates a new Logger with the given non-nil Handler.

slog.NewTextHandler.description: Creates a TextHandler that writes Records to w as line-delimited key=value pairs.

slog.NewJSONHandler.description: Creates a JSONHandler that writes Records to w as line-delimited JSON objects.

slog.SetDefault.description: Makes l the default Logger. It also updates the default logger used by the log package.

slog.Group.description: Returns an Attr for a Group Value. The caller must not subsequently mutate the slice. Use to nest attributes under a key.

slog.String.description: Returns an Attr for a string value. Used as typed attribute: slog.Info("msg", slog.String("name", val)).

slog.Int.signature: func slog.Int(key string, value int) slog.Attr
slog.Int.description: Returns an Attr for an int value.

slog.Any.signature: func slog.Any(key string, value any) slog.Attr
slog.Any.description: Returns an Attr for any value.

exec.Command.description: Returns the Cmd struct to execute the named program with the given arguments. It sets only the Path and Args in the returned structure. The returned Cmd's Args field is constructed from the command name followed by the elements of arg.

exec.CommandContext.signature: func exec.CommandContext(ctx context.Context, name string, arg ...string) *exec.Cmd
exec.CommandContext.description: Like Command but includes a context. The provided context is used to kill the process (by calling os.Process.Kill) if the context becomes done before the command completes on its own.

exec.LookPath.description: Searches for an executable named file in the directories named by the PATH environment variable. If file contains a slash, it is tried directly.

exec.Cmd.Run.description: Starts the specified command and waits for it to complete. The returned error is nil if the command runs, has no problems copying stdin/stdout/stderr, and exits with a zero exit status.

exec.Cmd.Start.description: Starts the specified command but does not wait for it to complete. The Wait method will return the exit code and release associated resources once the command exits.

exec.Cmd.Wait.description: Waits for the command to exit and waits for any copying to stdin or copying from stdout/stderr to complete. Must be called after Start.

exec.Cmd.Output.description: Runs the command and returns its standard output. Any returned error will usually be of type *ExitError. If c.Stderr is nil, Output populates ExitError.Stderr.

exec.Cmd.CombinedOutput.description: Runs the command and returns its combined standard output and standard error.

exec.Cmd.StdinPipe.description: Returns a pipe that will be connected to the command's standard input when the command starts. The pipe will be closed automatically after Wait sees the command exit or after calling Close.

exec.Cmd.StdoutPipe.description: Returns a pipe that will be connected to the command's standard output when the command starts. Wait will close the pipe after seeing the command exit.

signal.Notify.description: Causes package signal to relay incoming signals to c. If no signals are provided, all incoming signals will be relayed. Channel should be buffered: make(chan os.Signal, 1).

signal.NotifyContext.signature: func signal.NotifyContext(parent context.Context, signals ...os.Signal) (ctx context.Context, stop context.CancelFunc)
signal.NotifyContext.description: Returns a copy of the parent context that is marked done (its Done channel is closed) when one of the listed signals arrives, when the returned stop function is called, or when the parent context's Done channel is closed.

signal.Stop.description: Causes package signal to stop relaying incoming signals to c. It undoes the effect of all prior calls to Notify involving c.

signal.Reset.description: Undoes the effect of any prior calls to Notify for the provided signals. If no signals given, resets all.

net.Listen.description: Announces on the local network address. The network must be 'tcp', 'tcp4', 'tcp6', 'unix', or 'unixpacket'. For TCP, the address has the form 'host:port' or ':port'.

net.Dial.description: Connects to the address on the named network. Known networks are 'tcp', 'tcp4', 'tcp6', 'udp', 'udp4', 'udp6', 'ip', 'ip4', 'ip6', 'unix', 'unixgram', 'unixpacket'.

net.DialTimeout.signature: func net.DialTimeout(network, address string, timeout time.Duration) (net.Conn, error)
net.DialTimeout.description: Like Dial but with a timeout. The timeout includes name resolution, if required.

net.Dialer.signature: type net.Dialer struct { Timeout time.Duration; Deadline time.Time; LocalAddr Addr; ... }
net.Dialer.description: Contains options for connecting to an address. The zero value is equivalent to dialing without a Dialer. Use DialContext for context-aware dialing.

net.Dialer.DialContext.signature: func (*net.Dialer) DialContext(ctx context.Context, network, address string) (net.Conn, error)
net.Dialer.DialContext.description: Connects to the address on the named network using the provided context. The context controls the entire dial, including name resolution.

net.Listener.signature: type net.Listener interface { Accept() (Conn, error); Close() error; Addr() Addr }
net.Listener.description: A generic network listener. Multiple goroutines may invoke methods on a Listener simultaneously.

net.Conn.signature: type net.Conn interface { Read(b []byte) (n int, err error); Write(b []byte) (n int, err error); Close() error; ... }
net.Conn.description: A generic stream-oriented network connection. Multiple goroutines may invoke methods on a Conn simultaneously.

net.LookupHost.description: Looks up the given host using the local resolver. It returns a slice of that host's addresses.

net.LookupIP.description: Looks up IP addresses for the named host. Returns a slice of IP addresses.

net.SplitHostPort.signature: func net.SplitHostPort(hostport string) (host, port string, err error)
net.SplitHostPort.description: Splits a network address of the form 'host:port', 'host%zone:port', '[host]:port' or '[host%zone]:port' into host or host%zone and port.

net.JoinHostPort.description: Combines a host and a port into a network address of the form 'host:port'. If host contains a colon, as found in literal IPv6 addresses, then JoinHostPort returns '[host]:port'.

net.ParseCIDR.description: Parses s as a CIDR notation IP address and prefix length, like '192.0.2.0/24' or '2001:db8::/32'.

net.ParseIP.description: Parses s as an IP address, returning the result. The string s can be in dotted decimal ('192.0.2.1') or IPv6 ('2001:db8::1') form. Returns nil if s is not a valid IP.

net.IP.description: An IP address. It is a single IP address, a slice of bytes. Functions in this package accept either 4-byte (IPv4) or 16-byte (IPv6) slices.

net.TCPAddr.signature: type net.TCPAddr struct { IP IP; Port int; Zone string }
net.TCPAddr.description: Represents the address of a TCP end point.

net.ResolveTCPAddr.description: Returns an address of TCP end point. The network must be a TCP network name.

url.Parse.description: Parses a raw url into a URL structure. It is parsed in conformance with RFC 3986. Relative URLs are interpreted relative to the empty base.

url.ParseRequestURI.description: Parses rawURL into a URL structure. Expects an absolute URI or absolute path.

url.URL.signature: type url.URL struct { Scheme string; Opaque string; User *Userinfo; Host string; Path string; RawPath string; RawQuery string; Fragment string }
url.URL.description: Represents a parsed URL. The general form is: [scheme:][//[userinfo@]host][/]path[?query][#fragment].

url.URL.String.description: Reassembles the URL into a valid URL string.

url.URL.Query.description: Parses RawQuery and returns the corresponding values. It silently discards malformed value pairs.

url.URL.Hostname.description: Returns u.Host, stripping any valid port number if present. If the result is enclosed in square brackets (IPv6 literal), the brackets are stripped.

url.URL.Port.description: Returns the port part of u.Host, without the leading colon. If u.Host doesn't contain a valid numeric port, Port returns an empty string.

url.URL.JoinPath.description: Returns a new URL with the provided path elements joined to the existing path. Added in Go 1.19.

url.Values.description: Maps a string key to a list of values. It is typically used for query parameters and form values.

url.Values.Get.description: Gets the first value associated with the given key. Returns '' if no values.

url.Values.Set.description: Sets the key to value. Replaces any existing values.

url.Values.Add.description: Adds the value to key without removing existing values.

url.Values.Del.description: Deletes the values associated with key.

url.Values.Encode.description: Encodes the values into URL-encoded form ('bar=baz&foo=quux') sorted by key.

url.Values.Has.description: Checks whether a given key is set. Added in Go 1.17.

url.QueryEscape.description: Escapes the string so it can be safely placed inside a URL query.

url.QueryUnescape.description: Does the inverse of QueryEscape, converting %AB into the byte 0xAB.

url.PathEscape.description: Escapes the string so it can be safely placed inside a URL path segment, replacing special characters with %XX sequences.

math.Abs.description: Returns the absolute value of x.

math.Max.description: Returns the larger of x or y. For integer max, use the builtin max() (Go 1.21+).

math.Min.description: Returns the smaller of x or y. For integer min, use the builtin min() (Go 1.21+).

math.Ceil.description: Returns the least integer value greater than or equal to x.

math.Floor.description: Returns the greatest integer value less than or equal to x.

math.Round.description: Returns the nearest integer, rounding half away from zero.

math.Sqrt.description: Returns the square root of x.

math.Pow.description: Returns x**y, the base-x exponential of y.

math.Log.description: Returns the natural logarithm of x.

math.Log2.description: Returns the binary (base-2) logarithm of x.

math.Log10.description: Returns the decimal logarithm of x.

math.Inf.description: Returns positive or negative infinity depending on the sign of the argument.

math.IsNaN.description: Reports whether f is an IEEE 754 'not-a-number' value.

math.IsInf.signature: func math.IsInf(f float64, sign int) bool
math.IsInf.description: Reports whether f is positive or negative infinity, according to sign.

math.Mod.description: Returns the floating-point remainder of x/y. The magnitude of the result is less than y and its sign agrees with that of x.

math.Sin.description: Returns the sine of the radian argument x.

math.Cos.description: Returns the cosine of the radian argument x.

rand.Intn.description: Returns, as an int, a non-negative pseudo-random number in [0, n). Panics if n <= 0. In Go 1.22+, auto-seeded.

rand.Float64.description: Returns, as a float64, a pseudo-random number in [0.0, 1.0).

rand.Shuffle.signature: func rand.Shuffle(n int, swap func(i, j int))
rand.Shuffle.description: Pseudo-randomizes the order of elements using Fisher-Yates shuffle. n is the number of elements. swap swaps elements with indexes i and j.

rand.New.description: Returns a new Rand that uses random values from src to generate other random values.

rand.NewSource.description: Returns a new pseudo-random Source seeded with the given value.

reflect.TypeOf.description: Returns the reflection Type that represents the dynamic type of i. If i is a nil interface value, TypeOf returns nil.

reflect.ValueOf.description: Returns a new Value initialized to the concrete value stored in the interface i. ValueOf(nil) returns the zero Value.

reflect.DeepEqual.description: Reports whether x and y are 'deeply equal' — involves recursion through structs, arrays, slices, and maps. Commonly used in tests.

reflect.Type.signature: type reflect.Type interface { Name() string; Kind() Kind; NumField() int; Field(i int) StructField; NumMethod() int; ... }
reflect.Type.description: Representation of a Go type. Not all methods apply to all kinds of types.

reflect.Value.description: The reflection interface to a Go value. Zero Value represents no value. Methods include Interface(), Int(), String(), Bool(), Elem(), Set(), etc.

reflect.Value.Interface.description: Returns v's current value as an interface{}. Equivalent to: var i interface{} = (v's underlying value).

reflect.Value.Elem.description: Returns the value that the interface v contains or that the pointer v points to. Panics if kind is not Interface or Pointer.

reflect.Value.Kind.description: Returns v's Kind (Bool, Int, String, Struct, Ptr, Slice, Map, Func, Chan, Interface, etc.).

reflect.Value.Set.description: Assigns x to the value v. Panics if CanSet() is false. The value v must be addressable and not obtained by the use of unexported struct fields.

reflect.Value.CanSet.description: Reports whether the value of v can be changed. A Value obtained through unexported struct fields is not settable.

reflect.MakeSlice.signature: func reflect.MakeSlice(typ reflect.Type, len, cap int) reflect.Value
reflect.MakeSlice.description: Creates a new zero-initialized slice value for the specified slice type, with the given length and capacity.

reflect.MakeMap.description: Creates a new map with the specified type.

reflect.New.description: Returns a Value representing a pointer to a new zero value of the specified type.

reflect.Zero.description: Returns a Value representing the zero value for the specified type.

runtime.NumGoroutine.description: Returns the number of goroutines that currently exist.

runtime.NumCPU.description: Returns the number of logical CPUs usable by the current process.

runtime.GOMAXPROCS.description: Sets the maximum number of CPUs that can be executing simultaneously and returns the previous setting. If n < 1, it does not change the current setting.

runtime.Gosched.description: Yields the processor, allowing other goroutines to run. It does not suspend the current goroutine, so execution resumes automatically.

runtime.Goexit.description: Terminates the goroutine that calls it. No other goroutine is affected. Goexit runs all deferred functions before terminating the goroutine.

runtime.GC.description: Runs a garbage collection and blocks the caller until the garbage collection is complete.

runtime.Caller.signature: func runtime.Caller(skip int) (pc uintptr, file string, line int, ok bool)
runtime.Caller.description: Reports file and line number information about function invocations on the calling goroutine's stack. skip=0 is the caller of Caller.

runtime.Callers.description: Fills the slice pc with the return program counters of function invocations on the calling goroutine's stack.

runtime.Stack.description: Formats a stack trace of the calling goroutine into buf and returns the number of bytes written. If all is true, formats stack traces of all goroutines.

runtime.SetFinalizer.description: Sets the finalizer associated with obj to the provided finalizer function. When the garbage collector finds an unreachable block with an associated finalizer, it runs the finalizer in a separate goroutine.

runtime.GOOS.description: The running program's operating system target: 'darwin', 'freebsd', 'linux', 'windows', etc. Set at compile time.

runtime.GOARCH.description: The running program's architecture target: 'amd64', 'arm64', '386', 'arm', etc. Set at compile time.

runtime.Version.description: Returns the Go tree's version string, e.g. 'go1.21.0'.

runtime.MemStats.signature: type runtime.MemStats struct { Alloc uint64; TotalAlloc uint64; Sys uint64; NumGC uint32; HeapAlloc uint64; HeapInuse uint64; ... }
runtime.MemStats.description: Records statistics about the memory allocator.

runtime.ReadMemStats.description: Populates m with memory allocator statistics. Stops the world during the call.

testing.T.description: A type passed to Test functions to manage test state and support formatted test logs. A test ends when its Test function returns or calls any of the methods FailNow, Fatal, Fatalf, SkipNow, Skip, or Skipf.

testing.T.Run.signature: func (*testing.T) Run(name string, f func(t *testing.T)) bool
testing.T.Run.description: Runs f as a subtest of t called name. It runs f in a separate goroutine and blocks until f returns or calls t.Parallel.

testing.T.Error.description: Equivalent to Log followed by Fail. Marks the test as having failed but continues execution.

testing.T.Errorf.description: Equivalent to Logf followed by Fail. Marks the test as failed and logs with formatting.

testing.T.Fatal.description: Equivalent to Log followed by FailNow. Logs and stops the test immediately.

testing.T.Fatalf.description: Equivalent to Logf followed by FailNow. Logs with formatting and stops the test immediately.

testing.T.Log.description: Formats its arguments using default formatting and records the text in the error log. For tests, the text will be printed only if the test fails or the -test.v flag is set.

testing.T.Logf.description: Formats its arguments according to the format like Sprintf and records the text in the error log.

testing.T.Skip.description: Equivalent to Log followed by SkipNow. Marks the test as skipped and stops its execution.

testing.T.Skipf.description: Equivalent to Logf followed by SkipNow.

testing.T.Parallel.description: Signals that this test is to be run in parallel with (and only with) other parallel tests. When a test is run in parallel, it will be paused until its parent test function has returned.

testing.T.Helper.description: Marks the calling function as a test helper function. When printing file and line information, that function will be skipped. Helper may be called simultaneously from multiple goroutines.

testing.T.Cleanup.description: Registers a function to be called when the test and all its subtests complete. Cleanup functions are called in LIFO order.

testing.T.TempDir.description: Returns a temporary directory for the test to use. The directory is automatically removed when the test and all its subtests complete.

testing.T.Setenv.description: Calls os.Setenv(key, value) and uses Cleanup to restore the environment variable to its original value after the test. Cannot be used in parallel tests.

testing.T.Deadline.description: Reports the time at which the test binary will have exceeded the timeout specified by the -timeout flag. ok is false if -timeout is 0 (no timeout).

testing.B.description: A type passed to Benchmark functions. Manages benchmark timing and iteration count. b.N controls the number of iterations.

testing.B.Run.signature: func (*testing.B) Run(name string, f func(b *testing.B)) bool
testing.B.Run.description: Runs f as a sub-benchmark of b called name.

testing.B.ResetTimer.description: Zeroes the elapsed benchmark time and memory allocation counters and deletes user-reported metrics. Does not affect timer running state.

testing.B.StopTimer.description: Stops timing a test. Can be used to pause the timer while performing complex initialization.

testing.B.StartTimer.description: Starts timing a test. Called automatically before a benchmark starts, but can be used to resume timing after StopTimer.

testing.B.ReportAllocs.description: Enables malloc statistics for this benchmark. Equivalent to -test.benchmem.

testing.M.description: Used for TestMain functions. Provides a method Run() that runs the tests and returns an exit code. Must call os.Exit with the result.

sql.Open.description: Opens a database specified by its database driver name and a driver-specific data source name. Does not establish a connection — use Ping() to verify.

sql.DB.description: A database handle representing a pool of zero or more underlying connections. Safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines.

sql.DB.Query.description: Executes a query that returns rows, typically a SELECT.

sql.DB.QueryContext.signature: func (*sql.DB) QueryContext(ctx context.Context, query string, args ...any) (*sql.Rows, error)
sql.DB.QueryContext.description: Like Query but with a context for cancellation/timeout.

sql.DB.QueryRow.description: Executes a query that is expected to return at most one row. Always returns a non-nil value; errors deferred until Scan.

sql.DB.QueryRowContext.signature: func (*sql.DB) QueryRowContext(ctx context.Context, query string, args ...any) *sql.Row
sql.DB.QueryRowContext.description: Like QueryRow but with a context.

sql.DB.Exec.description: Executes a query without returning any rows. Typically used for INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE.

sql.DB.ExecContext.signature: func (*sql.DB) ExecContext(ctx context.Context, query string, args ...any) (sql.Result, error)
sql.DB.ExecContext.description: Like Exec but with a context.

sql.DB.Prepare.description: Creates a prepared statement for later queries or executions. Multiple queries or executions may run concurrently from the returned statement.

sql.DB.PrepareContext.signature: func (*sql.DB) PrepareContext(ctx context.Context, query string) (*sql.Stmt, error)
sql.DB.PrepareContext.description: Like Prepare but with a context.

sql.DB.Begin.description: Starts a transaction. The default isolation level is dependent on the driver.

sql.DB.BeginTx.description: Starts a transaction with the provided context and options.

sql.DB.Ping.description: Verifies a connection to the database is still alive, establishing a connection if necessary.

sql.DB.PingContext.description: Like Ping but with a context.

sql.DB.Close.description: Closes the database and prevents new queries from starting. Waits for queries that started processing to finish.

sql.DB.SetMaxOpenConns.description: Sets the maximum number of open connections to the database. If n <= 0, no limit. Default is 0 (unlimited).

sql.DB.SetMaxIdleConns.description: Sets the maximum number of connections in the idle connection pool. If n <= 0, no idle connections. Default is 2.

sql.DB.SetConnMaxLifetime.description: Sets the maximum amount of time a connection may be reused. If d <= 0, connections are not closed due to age.

sql.Rows.description: The result of a query. Its cursor starts before the first row. Use Next to advance from row to row.

sql.Rows.Next.description: Prepares the next row for reading with the Scan method. Returns true on success, false when no more rows or an error happened.

sql.Rows.Scan.description: Copies the columns in the current row into the values pointed at by dest. Number of values must match the number of columns.

sql.Rows.Close.description: Closes the Rows, preventing further enumeration. If Next is called and returns false, the Rows are closed automatically.

sql.Rows.Err.description: Returns the error, if any, encountered during iteration. Should be checked after a Next loop.

sql.Row.Scan.description: Copies the columns from the matched row into the values pointed at by dest. If no rows matched, returns ErrNoRows.

sql.Tx.description: An in-progress database transaction. Must end with a call to Commit or Rollback. After a call to Commit/Rollback, all operations fail with ErrTxDone.

sql.NullString.signature: type sql.NullString struct { String string; Valid bool }
sql.NullString.description: Represents a string that may be null. Implements Scanner and driver.Valuer.

sql.NullInt64.signature: type sql.NullInt64 struct { Int64 int64; Valid bool }
sql.NullInt64.description: Represents an int64 that may be null.

sql.NullBool.signature: type sql.NullBool struct { Bool bool; Valid bool }
sql.NullBool.description: Represents a bool that may be null.

sql.ErrNoRows.description: Returned by Row.Scan when QueryRow doesn't return a row. In such case, QueryRow returns a placeholder *Row value that defers this error until a Scan.

base64.StdEncoding.description: The standard base64 encoding, as defined in RFC 4648.

base64.URLEncoding.description: The alternate base64 encoding defined in RFC 4648. Typically used in URLs and file names.

base64.Encoding.EncodeToString.description: Returns the base64 encoding of src.

base64.Encoding.DecodeString.description: Returns the bytes represented by the base64 string s.

hex.EncodeToString.description: Returns the hexadecimal encoding of src.

hex.DecodeString.description: Returns the bytes represented by the hexadecimal string s.

xml.Marshal.description: Returns the XML encoding of v.

xml.MarshalIndent.signature: func xml.MarshalIndent(v any, prefix, indent string) ([]byte, error)
xml.MarshalIndent.description: Like Marshal but applies Indent to format the output.

xml.Unmarshal.description: Parses the XML-encoded data and stores the result in the value pointed to by v.

xml.NewDecoder.description: Creates a new XML parser reading from r.

xml.NewEncoder.description: Returns a new encoder that writes to w.

csv.NewReader.description: Returns a new Reader that reads from r.

csv.Reader.Read.description: Reads one record (a slice of fields) from r. Returns io.EOF at end of input.

csv.Reader.ReadAll.description: Reads all remaining records from r. Each record is a slice of fields.

csv.NewWriter.description: Returns a new Writer that writes to w.

csv.Writer.Write.description: Writes a single CSV record to w along with any necessary quoting.

csv.Writer.WriteAll.description: Writes multiple CSV records to w using Write and then calls Flush.

csv.Writer.Flush.description: Writes any buffered data to the underlying io.Writer.

flag.String.signature: func flag.String(name string, value string, usage string) *string
flag.String.description: Defines a string flag with specified name, default value, and usage string. The return value is the address of a string variable.

flag.Int.signature: func flag.Int(name string, value int, usage string) *int

flag.Bool.signature: func flag.Bool(name string, value bool, usage string) *bool

flag.Float64.signature: func flag.Float64(name string, value float64, usage string) *float64

flag.Duration.signature: func flag.Duration(name string, value time.Duration, usage string) *time.Duration

flag.StringVar.signature: func flag.StringVar(p *string, name string, value string, usage string)
flag.StringVar.description: Like String, but stores the flag value in the string variable pointed to by p.

flag.Parse.description: Parses the command-line flags from os.Args[1:]. Must be called after all flags are defined and before flags are accessed by the program.

flag.Arg.description: Returns the i'th command-line argument. Arg(0) is the first remaining argument after flags.

flag.NArg.description: Returns the number of arguments remaining after flags have been processed.

flag.NFlag.description: Returns the number of command-line flags that have been set.

flag.NewFlagSet.description: Returns a new, empty flag set with the specified name and error handling behavior.

hash.Hash.signature: type hash.Hash interface { io.Writer; Sum(b []byte) []byte; Reset(); Size() int; BlockSize() int }
hash.Hash.description: The common interface implemented by all hash functions. Implementations include sha256, sha512, md5, crc32, etc.

gzip.NewReader.description: Creates a new Reader reading the given reader. Remember to call Close when done.

gzip.NewWriter.description: Returns a new Writer. Writes to the returned writer are compressed and written to w. Remember to call Close to flush buffered data.

gzip.NewWriterLevel.signature: func gzip.NewWriterLevel(w io.Writer, level int) (*gzip.Writer, error)
gzip.NewWriterLevel.description: Like NewWriter but specifies the compression level: gzip.NoCompression, gzip.BestSpeed, gzip.BestCompression, gzip.DefaultCompression, gzip.HuffmanOnly.

zip.OpenReader.description: Opens a zip file for reading.

zip.NewReader.signature: func zip.NewReader(r io.ReaderAt, size int64) (*zip.Reader, error)
zip.NewReader.description: Returns a new Reader reading from r, which is assumed to have the given size in bytes.

zip.NewWriter.description: Returns a new Writer writing a zip file to w.

zip.Writer.Create.description: Adds a file to the zip file using the provided name. Returns a Writer to which the file contents should be written.

zip.Writer.Close.description: Finishes writing the zip file by writing the central directory. Does not close the underlying writer.

cmp.Compare.signature: func cmp.Compare[T cmp.Ordered](x, y T) int
cmp.Compare.description: Returns -1, 0, or +1 depending on whether x < y, x == y, or x > y. For floating-point types, NaN is treated as less than any non-NaN. Added in Go 1.21.

cmp.Less.signature: func cmp.Less[T cmp.Ordered](x, y T) bool
cmp.Less.description: Reports whether x is less than y. For floating-point types, NaN is considered less than any non-NaN.

cmp.Or.description: Returns the first non-zero value among vals, or the zero value of T if all are zero. Added in Go 1.22.

cmp.Ordered.description: A constraint that permits any ordered type: any integer, floating-point, or string type.

embed.FS.description: An embedded file system. Use //go:embed directives to populate. Implements fs.FS, fs.ReadDirFS, and fs.ReadFileFS.

embed.FS.Open.description: Opens the named file in the embedded file system.

embed.FS.ReadFile.description: Reads and returns the content of the named file.

embed.FS.ReadDir.description: Reads and returns the entire named directory.

fs.FS.signature: type fs.FS interface { Open(name string) (File, error) }
fs.FS.description: Provides access to a hierarchical file system. Implementations include os.DirFS(), embed.FS, zip.Reader, etc.

fs.File.signature: type fs.File interface { Stat() (FileInfo, error); Read([]byte) (int, error); Close() error }
fs.File.description: Provides access to a single file. The File interface is the minimum implementation required of the file.

fs.DirEntry.signature: type fs.DirEntry interface { Name() string; IsDir() bool; Type() FileMode; Info() (FileInfo, error) }
fs.DirEntry.description: An entry read from a directory.

fs.WalkDir.signature: func fs.WalkDir(fsys fs.FS, root string, fn fs.WalkDirFunc) error
fs.WalkDir.description: Walks the file tree rooted at root, calling fn for each file or directory including root.

fs.ReadFile.signature: func fs.ReadFile(fsys fs.FS, name string) ([]byte, error)
fs.ReadFile.description: Reads the named file from the file system fsys and returns its contents.

fs.ReadDir.signature: func fs.ReadDir(fsys fs.FS, name string) ([]fs.DirEntry, error)
fs.ReadDir.description: Reads the named directory and returns a list of directory entries sorted by filename.

fs.Sub.signature: func fs.Sub(fsys fs.FS, dir string) (fs.FS, error)
fs.Sub.description: Returns an FS corresponding to the subtree rooted at dir.

fs.Glob.signature: func fs.Glob(fsys fs.FS, pattern string) (matches []string, err error)
fs.Glob.description: Returns the names of all files matching pattern within fsys.

template.New.description: Allocates a new, undefined template with the given name.

template.ParseFiles.description: Creates a new Template and parses the template definitions from the named files. The returned template's name will be the base name of the first file.

template.ParseGlob.description: Creates a new Template and parses the template definitions from the files identified by the glob pattern.

template.Template.Execute.signature: func (*template.Template) Execute(wr io.Writer, data any) error
template.Template.Execute.description: Applies a parsed template to the specified data object and writes the output to wr.

template.Template.ExecuteTemplate.signature: func (*template.Template) ExecuteTemplate(wr io.Writer, name string, data any) error
template.Template.ExecuteTemplate.description: Applies the template associated with t that has the given name to the specified data object and writes the output to wr.

unicode.IsLetter.description: Reports whether the rune is a letter (category L).

unicode.IsDigit.description: Reports whether the rune is a decimal digit.

unicode.IsSpace.description: Reports whether the rune is a space character as defined by Unicode's White Space property.

unicode.IsUpper.description: Reports whether the rune is an upper case letter.

unicode.IsLower.description: Reports whether the rune is a lower case letter.

unicode.ToUpper.description: Maps the rune to upper case.

unicode.ToLower.description: Maps the rune to lower case.

httptest.NewServer.description: Starts and returns a new Server. The caller should call Close when finished to shut it down.

httptest.NewTLSServer.description: Starts and returns a new HTTPS Server. The caller should call Close when finished.

httptest.NewRequest.signature: func httptest.NewRequest(method, target string, body io.Reader) *http.Request
httptest.NewRequest.description: Returns a new incoming server Request, suitable for passing to an http.Handler for testing.

httptest.NewRecorder.description: Returns an initialized ResponseRecorder. It implements http.ResponseWriter for capturing responses in tests.

httptest.Server.signature: type httptest.Server struct { URL string; Listener net.Listener; ... }
httptest.Server.description: A test HTTP server listening on a system-chosen port for use in end-to-end HTTP tests.

httputil.NewSingleHostReverseProxy.description: Returns a new ReverseProxy that routes URLs to the scheme, host, and base path in target.

httputil.DumpRequest.description: Returns the given request in its HTTP/1.x wire representation. Used for debugging.

httputil.DumpResponse.description: Like DumpRequest but for HTTP responses.

mime.TypeByExtension.description: Returns the MIME type associated with the file extension ext. Extension should include the dot: '.html'. Returns '' if ext is unknown.

mime.ParseMediaType.signature: func mime.ParseMediaType(v string) (mediatype string, params map[string]string, err error)
mime.ParseMediaType.description: Parses a media type value and any optional parameters, per RFC 2045 and RFC 2616.

mime.FormatMediaType.signature: func mime.FormatMediaType(t string, param map[string]string) string
mime.FormatMediaType.description: Serializes a media type, lowercased, and its parameters as a MIME media type conforming to RFC 2045.

os.DirFS.description: Returns a file system (an fs.FS) for the tree of files rooted at the directory dir. Useful for abstracting file operations.

os.Truncate.signature: func os.Truncate(name string, size int64) error
os.Truncate.description: Changes the size of the named file. If the file is a symbolic link, it changes the size of the link's target.

os.FileInfo.signature: type os.FileInfo interface { Name() string; Size() int64; Mode() FileMode; ModTime() time.Time; IsDir() bool; Sys() any }
os.FileInfo.description: Describes a file and is returned by Stat and Lstat.

os.FileMode.description: Represents a file's mode and permission bits. Constants: ModeDir, ModeSymlink, ModeNamedPipe, ModeSocket, ModeSetuid, ModeSetgid, ModeCharDevice, ModeSticky, ModeIrregular, ModeType, ModePerm.

os.DirEntry.signature: type os.DirEntry interface { Name() string; IsDir() bool; Type() FileMode; Info() (FileInfo, error) }
os.DirEntry.description: An entry read from a directory (via ReadDir or WalkDir).

os.ErrNotExist.description: Indicates that a file does not exist. Use errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) for checking.

os.ErrExist.description: Indicates that a file already exists.

os.PathError.signature: type os.PathError struct { Op string; Path string; Err error }
os.PathError.description: Records an error and the operation and path that caused it. Most os functions return *PathError on error.

os.File.Seek.signature: func (*os.File) Seek(offset int64, whence int) (ret int64, err error)
os.File.Seek.description: Sets the offset for the next Read or Write on file. whence: 0=start, 1=current, 2=end. Use io.SeekStart, io.SeekCurrent, io.SeekEnd.

os.File.Stat.description: Returns the FileInfo for the file.

os.File.Sync.description: Commits the current contents of the file to stable storage (fsync).

os.File.WriteString.signature: func (*os.File) WriteString(s string) (n int, err error)
os.File.WriteString.description: Writes the string s to the file. Returns the number of bytes written and any error.

os.File.ReadDir.description: Reads the contents of the directory. If n > 0, returns at most n entries; if n <= 0, returns all entries.

os.File.Chmod.description: Changes the mode of the file.

io.ReadFull.signature: func io.ReadFull(r io.Reader, buf []byte) (n int, err error)
io.ReadFull.description: Reads exactly len(buf) bytes from r into buf. Returns the number of bytes read and io.ErrUnexpectedEOF if fewer bytes were read. Returns io.EOF only if no bytes were read.

io.NewSectionReader.signature: func io.NewSectionReader(r io.ReaderAt, off int64, n int64) *io.SectionReader
io.NewSectionReader.description: Returns a SectionReader starting at byte offset off, reading up to n bytes.

io.ReadAtLeast.signature: func io.ReadAtLeast(r io.Reader, buf []byte, min int) (n int, err error)
io.ReadAtLeast.description: Reads from r into buf until it has read at least min bytes. Returns the number of bytes read and io.ErrUnexpectedEOF if fewer than min were read.

io.ByteReader.description: The interface that wraps the ReadByte method. Reads and returns the next byte.

io.RuneReader.signature: type io.RuneReader interface { ReadRune() (r rune, size int, err error) }
io.RuneReader.description: The interface that wraps the ReadRune method.

io.StringWriter.signature: type io.StringWriter interface { WriteString(s string) (n int, err error) }
io.StringWriter.description: The interface that wraps the WriteString method.

io.SeekStart.description: Seek relative to the origin (beginning) of the file.

io.SeekCurrent.description: Seek relative to the current offset.

io.SeekEnd.description: Seek relative to the end of the file.

strings.IndexAny.description: Returns the index of the first instance of any Unicode code point from chars in s, or -1 if not present.

http.Server.ListenAndServe.description: Listens on the TCP network address srv.Addr and calls Serve to handle requests.

http.Server.ListenAndServeTLS.description: Listens on the address and calls ServeTLS to handle HTTPS requests.

http.Server.Shutdown.description: Gracefully shuts down the server without interrupting any active connections. Closes listeners, then waits for all connections to close. If the context expires before shutdown completes, returns the context's error.

http.ServeMux.HandleFunc.description: Registers the handler function for the given pattern. In Go 1.22+, supports method and path parameters: 'GET /users/{id}'.

http.ServeMux.Handle.description: Registers the handler for the given pattern.

http.FS.description: Converts an fs.FS to an http.FileSystem for use with http.FileServer.

http.TimeoutHandler.signature: func http.TimeoutHandler(h http.Handler, dt time.Duration, msg string) http.Handler
http.TimeoutHandler.description: Returns a Handler that runs h with the given time limit. If h exceeds the time limit, the handler replies with 503 and the given message.

http.Request.Context.description: Returns the request's context. Always non-nil and defaults to background context for clients. For incoming server requests, the context is canceled when the client's connection closes or the request is canceled.

http.Request.WithContext.description: Returns a shallow copy of r with its context changed to ctx.

http.Request.PathValue.description: Returns the value for the named path wildcard in the ServeMux pattern that matched the request. Added in Go 1.22.

http.Request.FormValue.description: Returns the first value for the named component of the query or POST/PUT body. URL query parameters take precedence over form body.

http.Request.ParseForm.description: Populates r.Form and r.PostForm. For POST/PUT/PATCH requests, it reads the request body, parses it as a form, and puts results into both r.PostForm and r.Form.

http.Request.ParseMultipartForm.description: Parses multipart form data. Up to maxMemory bytes stored in memory; remainder stored to temp files.

http.Request.FormFile.description: Returns the first file for the provided form key. Calls ParseMultipartForm if not already called.

http.StatusMethodNotAllowed.description: HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed status code.

http.StatusInternalServerError.description: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error status code.

reflect.Indirect.description: Returns the value that v points to. If v is not a pointer, Indirect returns v itself.

reflect.Copy.description: Copies the contents of src into dst until either dst is full or src is exhausted. Returns the number of elements copied.

reflect.Append.description: Appends the values x to a slice s and returns the resulting slice.

reflect.SliceOf.description: Returns the slice type with element type t. For example, if t is int, SliceOf(t) returns []int.

reflect.MapOf.description: Returns the map type with the given key and element types.

reflect.PointerTo.description: Returns the pointer type with element t. For example, if t is Foo, PointerTo(t) is *Foo.

reflect.Value.IsNil.description: Reports whether its argument v is nil. The argument must be a chan, func, interface, map, pointer, or slice; if it's not, IsNil panics.

reflect.Value.IsZero.description: Reports whether v is the zero value for its type. Panics if v is invalid.

reflect.Value.NumField.description: Returns the number of fields in a struct v. Panics if Kind is not Struct.

reflect.Value.Field.description: Returns the i'th field of the struct v. Panics if v's Kind is not Struct or i is out of range.

reflect.Value.FieldByName.description: Returns the struct field with the given name. Returns the zero Value if no field was found.

reflect.Value.Call.description: Calls the function v with the input arguments in. Panics if v's Kind is not Func.

reflect.Value.Len.description: Returns the length of v. Panics unless Kind is Array, Chan, Map, Slice, or String.
