reflect.Value.MapKeys.description: Returns a slice containing all the keys present in the map, in unspecified order.

reflect.Value.MapIndex.description: Returns the value associated with key in the map v.

reflect.StructTag.description: A struct field's tag string. By convention, tag strings are optionally space-separated key:"value" pairs. Each key is a non-empty string. Use Get or Lookup to extract values.

reflect.StructTag.Get.description: Returns the value associated with key in the tag string. Returns empty string if key not present.

reflect.StructTag.Lookup.signature: func (reflect.StructTag) Lookup(key string) (value string, ok bool)
reflect.StructTag.Lookup.description: Returns the value associated with key and whether the key was found.

testing.Short.description: Reports whether the -test.short flag is set. Use to skip long-running tests.

testing.Verbose.description: Reports whether the -test.v flag is set.

testing.F.description: Type passed to fuzz test functions. Use f.Add to seed the corpus and f.Fuzz to run the fuzz target. Added in Go 1.18.

testing.F.Add.description: Adds seed corpus entries for the fuzz test. Each argument becomes one element of the initial corpus.

testing.F.Fuzz.description: Runs the fuzz function. ff must be a function of the form func(t *testing.T, args ...) where types match those in Add calls.

testing.T.FailNow.description: Marks the test as failed and stops its execution. Does not stop other tests.

testing.T.Fail.description: Marks the test as failed but continues execution.

testing.T.Failed.description: Reports whether the test has been marked as failed.

testing.T.Name.description: Returns the name of the running test or benchmark.

testing.T.Skipped.description: Reports whether the test was skipped.

time.FixedZone.description: Returns a Location that always uses the given zone name and offset. offset is in seconds east of UTC.

time.Location.description: Maps time instants to the zone in use at that time. UTC and Local are pre-defined Locations.

time.Month.description: Specifies a month of the year (January = 1, ..., December = 12).

time.Weekday.description: Specifies a day of the week (Sunday = 0, ..., Saturday = 6).

time.Time.Month.description: Returns the month of the year.

time.Time.Day.description: Returns the day of the month.

time.Time.Hour.description: Returns the hour within the day (0-23).

time.Time.Minute.description: Returns the minute offset within the hour (0-59).

time.Time.Second.description: Returns the second offset within the minute (0-59).

time.Time.Weekday.description: Returns the day of the week.

time.Time.AppendFormat.description: Like Format but appends the textual representation to b and returns the extended buffer.

time.Time.MarshalJSON.description: Implements the json.Marshaler interface. Encodes as RFC 3339 string.

time.Time.UnmarshalJSON.description: Implements the json.Unmarshaler interface. Expects RFC 3339 string.

time.RFC3339.description: RFC 3339 date-time format, the most common format for APIs and JSON.

time.DateTime.description: A common date-time layout. Added in Go 1.20.

time.DateOnly.description: A common date-only layout. Added in Go 1.20.

time.TimeOnly.description: A common time-only layout. Added in Go 1.20.

json.Decoder.UseNumber.description: Causes the Decoder to unmarshal a number into a json.Number instead of as a float64.

json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields.description: Causes Decode to return an error when the destination is a struct and the JSON contains object keys which do not match any non-ignored struct fields.

json.Encoder.SetIndent.description: Instructs the encoder to format each subsequent encoded value as if indented by the package-level MarshalIndent(v, prefix, indent).

json.Encoder.SetEscapeHTML.description: Specifies whether problematic HTML characters should be escaped inside JSON quoted strings. Default is true.

unsafe.Pointer.description: Represents a pointer to an arbitrary type. Can be converted to/from any pointer type and uintptr. Four special operations: (1) *T to unsafe.Pointer, (2) unsafe.Pointer to *T, (3) unsafe.Pointer to uintptr, (4) uintptr to unsafe.Pointer.

unsafe.Sizeof.description: Returns the size in bytes of the value represented by x. Does not include any memory possibly referenced by x.

unsafe.Alignof.description: Returns the alignment required for element type v. Same as reflect.TypeOf(x).Align().

unsafe.Offsetof.description: Returns the field offset within the struct. Argument must be structValue.field form.

unsafe.Add.signature: func unsafe.Add(ptr unsafe.Pointer, len IntegerType) unsafe.Pointer
unsafe.Add.description: Adds len to ptr and returns the updated pointer. Equivalent to unsafe.Pointer(uintptr(ptr) + uintptr(len)). Added in Go 1.17.

unsafe.Slice.description: Returns a slice whose underlying array starts at ptr and whose length and capacity are len. Added in Go 1.17.

unsafe.SliceData.description: Returns a pointer to the underlying array of the argument slice. Added in Go 1.20.

unsafe.String.description: Returns a string value whose underlying bytes start at ptr and whose length is len. Added in Go 1.20.

unsafe.StringData.description: Returns a pointer to the underlying bytes of the string. Added in Go 1.20.

template.Template.description: Representation of a parsed template. The *parse.Tree field is exported only for use by html/template.

template.Template.Parse.description: Parses the template text. If the template body was already defined, the new text replaces it.

template.Template.Funcs.description: Adds the elements of the argument map to the template's function map. Must be called before the template is parsed.

template.FuncMap.description: Defines the mapping of names to functions. Each value must be a function with a single return value or two return values (the second an error).

template.Must.description: A helper that wraps a call to a function returning (*Template, error) and panics if the error is non-nil. Used for variable initializations.

template.Template.Option.description: Sets options for the template. Options: 'missingkey=zero', 'missingkey=error', 'missingkey=default' (same as zero), 'missingkey=invalid'.

template.Template.Lookup.description: Returns the template with the given name that is associated with t, or nil if not found.

template.Template.Name.description: Returns the name of the template.

template.Template.Templates.description: Returns a slice of the templates associated with t, including t itself.

binary.Read.signature: func binary.Read(r io.Reader, order binary.ByteOrder, data any) error
binary.Read.description: Reads structured binary data from r into data. Data must be a pointer to a fixed-size value or a slice of fixed-size values.

binary.Write.signature: func binary.Write(w io.Writer, order binary.ByteOrder, data any) error
binary.Write.description: Writes the binary representation of data into w. Data must be a fixed-size value or a slice of fixed-size values, or a pointer to such data.

binary.BigEndian.description: Big-endian byte order implementation of ByteOrder and AppendByteOrder.

binary.LittleEndian.description: Little-endian byte order implementation of ByteOrder and AppendByteOrder.

binary.ByteOrder.signature: type binary.ByteOrder interface { Uint16([]byte) uint16; Uint32([]byte) uint32; Uint64([]byte) uint64; PutUint16([]byte, uint16); PutUint32([]byte, uint32); PutUint64([]byte, uint64); String() string }
binary.ByteOrder.description: Specifies how to convert byte slices into 16-, 32-, or 64-bit unsigned integers.

binary.PutUvarint.description: Encodes a uint64 into buf as a variable-length integer. Returns the number of bytes written.

binary.Uvarint.description: Decodes a uint64 from buf, returning the value and the number of bytes read (> 0). If an error occurred, the value is 0 and the number of bytes n is <= 0.

binary.PutVarint.description: Encodes an int64 into buf as a variable-length integer. Returns the number of bytes written.

binary.Varint.description: Decodes an int64 from buf, returning the value and the number of bytes read.

binary.Size.description: Returns how many bytes Write would generate to encode the value v. Returns -1 if v is not a fixed-size value or slice.

gob.NewEncoder.description: Returns a new encoder that will transmit on the io.Writer.

gob.NewDecoder.description: Returns a new decoder that reads from the io.Reader.

gob.Encoder.Encode.description: Transmits the data item represented by the empty interface value, guaranteeing that all necessary type information has been transmitted first.

gob.Decoder.Decode.description: Reads the next value from the input stream and stores it in the data represented by the empty interface value. e must be a pointer.

gob.Register.description: Records a type, identified by a value for that type, under its internal type name. Required for encoding/decoding interface values.

gob.RegisterName.description: Like Register but uses the provided name rather than the type's default.

pem.Encode.description: Writes the PEM encoding of b to out.

pem.Decode.description: Finds the next PEM formatted block (certificate, private key etc) in the input. Returns the block and remainder of the input.

pem.EncodeToMemory.description: Returns the PEM encoding of b.

pem.Block.signature: type pem.Block struct { Type string; Headers map[string]string; Bytes []byte }
pem.Block.description: Represents a PEM encoded structure. Type is the header e.g. 'RSA PRIVATE KEY'. Bytes contains the decoded bytes.

cipher.Block.signature: type cipher.Block interface { BlockSize() int; Encrypt(dst, src []byte); Decrypt(dst, src []byte) }
cipher.Block.description: Represents a block cipher. Provides encryption and decryption of individual blocks.

cipher.AEAD.signature: type cipher.AEAD interface { NonceSize() int; Overhead() int; Seal(dst, nonce, plaintext, additionalData []byte) []byte; Open(dst, nonce, ciphertext, additionalData []byte) ([]byte, error) }
cipher.AEAD.description: Authenticated Encryption with Associated Data. GCM is the most common AEAD mode.

cipher.NewGCM.description: Returns the given 128-bit block cipher wrapped in Galois Counter Mode with the standard nonce length (12 bytes).

cipher.NewCBCEncrypter.description: Returns a cipher.BlockMode which encrypts in cipher block chaining mode using the given Block and initialization vector.

cipher.NewCBCDecrypter.description: Returns a cipher.BlockMode which decrypts in cipher block chaining mode using the given Block and initialization vector.

cipher.BlockMode.signature: type cipher.BlockMode interface { BlockSize() int; CryptBlocks(dst, src []byte) }
cipher.BlockMode.description: Represents a block cipher running in a block-based mode (CBC, ECB, etc).

cipher.Stream.description: Represents a stream cipher (CFB, OFB, CTR).

cipher.NewCTR.description: Returns a Stream which encrypts/decrypts using the given Block in counter mode.

cipher.NewCFBEncrypter.description: Returns a Stream which encrypts with cipher feedback mode.

cipher.NewCFBDecrypter.description: Returns a Stream which decrypts with cipher feedback mode.

cipher.NewOFB.description: Returns a Stream that encrypts/decrypts using the block cipher in output feedback mode.

rsa.GenerateKey.signature: func rsa.GenerateKey(random io.Reader, bits int) (*rsa.PrivateKey, error)
rsa.GenerateKey.description: Generates an RSA keypair of the given bit size using the random source random (typically crypto/rand.Reader).

rsa.EncryptOAEP.signature: func rsa.EncryptOAEP(hash hash.Hash, random io.Reader, pub *rsa.PublicKey, msg []byte, label []byte) ([]byte, error)
rsa.EncryptOAEP.description: Encrypts the given message with RSA-OAEP.

rsa.DecryptOAEP.signature: func rsa.DecryptOAEP(hash hash.Hash, random io.Reader, priv *rsa.PrivateKey, ciphertext []byte, label []byte) ([]byte, error)

rsa.SignPKCS1v15.signature: func rsa.SignPKCS1v15(random io.Reader, priv *rsa.PrivateKey, hash crypto.Hash, hashed []byte) ([]byte, error)
rsa.SignPKCS1v15.description: Calculates the signature of hashed using RSASSA-PKCS1-V1_5-SIGN from RSA PKCS #1 v1.5.

rsa.VerifyPKCS1v15.signature: func rsa.VerifyPKCS1v15(pub *rsa.PublicKey, hash crypto.Hash, hashed []byte, sig []byte) error
rsa.VerifyPKCS1v15.description: Verifies an RSA PKCS #1 v1.5 signature.

rsa.PrivateKey.signature: type rsa.PrivateKey struct { PublicKey; D *big.Int; Primes []*big.Int; ... }

rsa.PublicKey.signature: type rsa.PublicKey struct { N *big.Int; E int }
rsa.PublicKey.description: Represents the public part of an RSA key.

rsa.SignPSS.signature: func rsa.SignPSS(rand io.Reader, priv *rsa.PrivateKey, hash crypto.Hash, digest []byte, opts *rsa.PSSOptions) ([]byte, error)
rsa.SignPSS.description: Calculates the signature of digest using PSS.

rsa.VerifyPSS.signature: func rsa.VerifyPSS(pub *rsa.PublicKey, hash crypto.Hash, hashed []byte, sig []byte, opts *rsa.PSSOptions) error

ecdsa.GenerateKey.signature: func ecdsa.GenerateKey(c elliptic.Curve, rand io.Reader) (*ecdsa.PrivateKey, error)
ecdsa.GenerateKey.description: Generates a new ECDSA private key for the specified curve.

ecdsa.Sign.signature: func ecdsa.Sign(rand io.Reader, priv *ecdsa.PrivateKey, hash []byte) (r, s *big.Int, err error)
ecdsa.Sign.description: Signs the hash with the private key, producing a pair of integers as signature.

ecdsa.Verify.signature: func ecdsa.Verify(pub *ecdsa.PublicKey, hash []byte, r, s *big.Int) bool
ecdsa.Verify.description: Verifies the signature of hash using the public key.

ecdsa.SignASN1.signature: func ecdsa.SignASN1(rand io.Reader, priv *ecdsa.PrivateKey, hash []byte) ([]byte, error)
ecdsa.SignASN1.description: Signs a hash (which should be the result of hashing a larger message) using the private key, producing an ASN.1 DER encoded signature.

ecdsa.VerifyASN1.description: Verifies the ASN.1 encoded signature, sig, of a hash using the public key, pub.

ecdsa.PublicKey.signature: type ecdsa.PublicKey struct { elliptic.Curve; X, Y *big.Int }
ecdsa.PublicKey.description: Represents the public half of an ECDSA key.

ed25519.GenerateKey.description: Generates a public/private key pair using entropy from rand. If rand is nil, crypto/rand.Reader will be used.

ed25519.Sign.description: Signs the message with privateKey and returns a 64-byte signature.

ed25519.Verify.signature: func ed25519.Verify(publicKey ed25519.PublicKey, message, sig []byte) bool
ed25519.Verify.description: Reports whether sig is a valid signature of message by publicKey.

ed25519.PublicKey.description: The public half of an Ed25519 key pair. 32 bytes.

ed25519.PrivateKey.description: The private half of an Ed25519 key pair. 64 bytes.

subtle.ConstantTimeCompare.description: Returns 1 if the two slices x and y have equal contents and 0 otherwise. The time taken is a function only of the length of the slices and is independent of the contents.

subtle.ConstantTimeSelect.signature: func subtle.ConstantTimeSelect(v, x, y int) int
subtle.ConstantTimeSelect.description: Returns x if v == 1, and y if v == 0. Its behavior is undefined if v takes any other value.

subtle.ConstantTimeEq.description: Returns 1 if x == y and 0 otherwise.

subtle.XORBytes.description: Sets dst[i] = x[i] ^ y[i] for all i < n = min(len(x), len(y)), returning n. Added in Go 1.20.

elliptic.P256.description: Returns a Curve which implements NIST P-256 (secp256r1, prime256v1).

elliptic.P384.description: Returns a Curve which implements NIST P-384 (secp384r1).

elliptic.P521.description: Returns a Curve which implements NIST P-521 (secp521r1).

crypto.Hash.description: Identifies a cryptographic hash function. Constants: MD5, SHA1, SHA224, SHA256, SHA384, SHA512, SHA512_224, SHA512_256.

init.description: Each source file can define its own init function. The init function takes no arguments and returns no value. Multiple init functions can exist in a single package and even in a single file. They are executed automatically when the package is initialized, before main.

heap.Init.description: Establishes the heap invariants required by the Interface methods. O(n) where n = h.Len(). Must be called before Push/Pop if the heap is not already valid.

heap.Push.description: Pushes the element x onto the heap. Complexity is O(log n).

heap.Pop.description: Removes and returns the minimum element (according to Less) from the heap. Complexity is O(log n). Equivalent to Remove(h, 0).

heap.Fix.description: Re-establishes the heap ordering after the element at index i has changed its value. O(log n).

heap.Remove.signature: func heap.Remove(h heap.Interface, i int) any
heap.Remove.description: Removes and returns the element at index i from the heap. Complexity is O(log n).

heap.Interface.signature: type heap.Interface interface { sort.Interface; Push(x any); Pop() any }
heap.Interface.description: Describes the requirements for a type using the heap package. Embeds sort.Interface and adds Push/Pop methods.

list.New.description: Returns an initialized doubly linked list.

list.List.description: Represents a doubly linked list. The zero value is a usable empty list.

list.Element.description: Element of a linked list. Value holds the value stored with this element.

list.List.PushBack.description: Inserts a new element at the back of list l and returns the element.

list.List.PushFront.description: Inserts a new element at the front of list l and returns the element.

list.List.Remove.description: Removes e from l if e is an element of list l. Returns the value e.Value.

list.List.Front.description: Returns the first element of list l or nil if the list is empty.

list.List.Back.description: Returns the last element of list l or nil if the list is empty.

list.List.Len.description: Returns the number of elements of list l. Complexity: O(1).

list.List.InsertBefore.description: Inserts a new element with value v before mark and returns the new element.

list.List.InsertAfter.description: Inserts a new element with value v after mark and returns the new element.

list.List.MoveToFront.description: Moves element e to the front of list l.

list.List.MoveToBack.description: Moves element e to the back of list l.

list.Element.Next.description: Returns the next list element or nil.

list.Element.Prev.description: Returns the previous list element or nil.

ring.New.description: Creates a ring of n elements. The value of each element in the ring is nil.

ring.Ring.description: An element of a circular list (ring). Rings do not have a beginning or end; a pointer to any ring element serves as reference to the entire ring.

ring.Ring.Do.description: Calls function f on each element of the ring, in forward order.

ring.Ring.Link.description: Connects ring r with ring s such that r.Next() becomes s and returns the original r.Next(). If r and s point to the same ring, inserting s between r and r.Next() removes the elements between r and s from the ring.

ring.Ring.Unlink.description: Removes n % r.Len() elements from the ring, starting at r.Next(). Returns the removed sub-ring.

ring.Ring.Len.description: Computes the number of elements in ring r. O(n).

ring.Ring.Move.description: Moves n % r.Len() elements forward (n > 0) or backward (n < 0) in the ring and returns that ring element.

tar.NewReader.description: Creates a new Reader reading from r.

tar.NewWriter.description: Creates a new Writer writing to w.

tar.Reader.Next.description: Advances to the next entry in the tar archive. Returns io.EOF at the end of the archive.

tar.Writer.WriteHeader.description: Writes hdr and prepares to accept the file's contents. WriteHeader calls Flush if ready.

tar.Writer.Write.description: Writes to the current file in the tar archive. Returns ErrWriteTooLong if more than hdr.Size bytes are written after WriteHeader.

tar.Writer.Close.description: Closes the tar archive by flushing the padding and writing the footer.

tar.Header.signature: type tar.Header struct { Typeflag byte; Name string; Linkname string; Size int64; Mode int64; Uid int; Gid int; ModTime time.Time; ... }
tar.Header.description: Represents a single header in a tar archive. Some fields may not be populated.

tar.FileInfoHeader.signature: func tar.FileInfoHeader(fi os.FileInfo, link string) (*tar.Header, error)
tar.FileInfoHeader.description: Creates a partially-populated Header from the fi. If fi describes a symlink, FileInfoHeader records link as the link target.

flate.NewReader.description: Returns a new ReadCloser that can be used to read the uncompressed version of r (raw DEFLATE stream).

flate.NewWriter.signature: func flate.NewWriter(w io.Writer, level int) (*flate.Writer, error)
flate.NewWriter.description: Returns a new Writer compressing data at the given level. Following zlib, levels range from 1 (BestSpeed) to 9 (BestCompression); higher levels run slower but compress better. Level 0 (NoCompression) writes uncompressed. Level -1 (DefaultCompression) is equivalent to level 6.

zlib.NewReader.description: Creates a new ReadCloser. Reads from the returned ReadCloser read and decompress data from r (zlib format).

zlib.NewWriter.description: Creates a new Writer. Writes to the returned Writer are compressed and written to w.

zlib.NewWriterLevel.signature: func zlib.NewWriterLevel(w io.Writer, level int) (*zlib.Writer, error)
zlib.NewWriterLevel.description: Like NewWriter but specifies the compression level.

path.Join.description: Joins any number of path elements into a single path, separating them with slashes. Empty elements are ignored. Uses forward slashes (for URL/unix paths). For OS paths, use path/filepath.Join.

path.Base.description: Returns the last element of path. If the path is empty, Base returns '.'. If the path consists entirely of slashes, Base returns '/'.

path.Dir.description: Returns all but the last element of path, typically the path's directory.

path.Ext.description: Returns the file name extension used by path. The extension is the suffix beginning at the final dot in the last element of path.

path.Clean.description: Returns the shortest path name equivalent to path by purely lexical processing.

path.Match.signature: func path.Match(pattern, name string) (matched bool, err error)
path.Match.description: Reports whether name matches the shell pattern. The pattern syntax is the same as in filepath.Match.

path.Split.description: Splits path immediately following the final slash, separating it into a directory and file name component.

path.IsAbs.description: Reports whether the path is absolute.

user.Lookup.description: Looks up a user by username.

user.LookupId.description: Looks up a user by userid.

user.LookupGroup.description: Looks up a group by name.

user.LookupGroupId.description: Looks up a group by groupid.

user.User.signature: type user.User struct { Uid string; Gid string; Username string; Name string; HomeDir string }

user.Group.signature: type user.Group struct { Gid string; Name string }

debug.SetGCPercent.description: Sets the garbage collection target percentage: a collection is triggered when the ratio of heap growth to remaining live data is at the target percentage. A negative value disables GC. Returns the previous setting.

debug.ReadBuildInfo.description: Returns the build information embedded in the running binary. The info is available only in binaries built with module support.

debug.Stack.description: Returns a formatted stack trace of the goroutine that calls it. Useful for logging/debugging.

debug.FreeOSMemory.description: Forces a garbage collection followed by an attempt to return as much memory to the operating system as possible.

debug.SetMaxStack.description: Sets the maximum amount of memory that can be used by a single goroutine stack. Returns the previous setting.

debug.SetMaxThreads.description: Sets the maximum number of operating system threads the Go program can use. Returns the previous setting.

debug.SetPanicOnFault.description: Controls the runtime's behavior when a program faults with an unexpected (non-nil) address. Returns the previous setting.

debug.BuildInfo.signature: type debug.BuildInfo struct { GoVersion string; Path string; Main Module; Deps []*Module; Settings []BuildSetting }
debug.BuildInfo.description: Represents the build information read from a Go binary.

debug.SetMemoryLimit.description: Sets the runtime's soft memory limit. Returns the previous limit. Added in Go 1.19.

expvar.NewInt.description: Creates a new expvar.Int, registers it under name, and returns it.

expvar.NewFloat.description: Creates a new expvar.Float, registers it under name, and returns it.

expvar.NewMap.description: Creates a new expvar.Map, registers it under name, and returns it.

expvar.NewString.description: Creates a new expvar.String, registers it under name, and returns it.

expvar.Publish.description: Declares a named exported variable. Panics if name has already been registered.

expvar.Handler.description: Returns the expvar HTTP Handler. This is usually registered at /debug/vars.

expvar.Do.description: Calls f for each exported variable. The global variable map is locked during the iteration; don't call Do from within f.

big.NewInt.description: Allocates and returns a new Int set to x.

big.Int.description: Represents a signed multi-precision integer. The zero value is 0.

big.Int.Add.description: Sets z to the sum x+y and returns z.

big.Int.Sub.description: Sets z to the difference x-y and returns z.

big.Int.Mul.description: Sets z to the product x*y and returns z.

big.Int.Div.description: Sets z to x/y (integer division, truncated toward zero). Panics if y is 0.

big.Int.Mod.description: Sets z to x mod y. Panics if y is 0.

big.Int.Cmp.description: Compares x and y. Returns -1, 0, or +1 if x < y, x == y, or x > y.

big.Int.SetString.signature: func (*big.Int) SetString(s string, base int) (*big.Int, bool)
big.Int.SetString.description: Sets z to the value of s, interpreted in the given base, and returns z and a success flag.

big.Int.String.description: Returns the decimal string representation of x.

big.Int.Int64.description: Returns the int64 representation of x. The result is undefined if x cannot be represented in an int64.

big.Int.Bytes.description: Returns the absolute value of x as a big-endian byte slice.

big.Int.SetBytes.description: Sets z to the value of the unsigned integer stored in buf, interpreted as a big-endian unsigned integer.

big.Int.Exp.description: Sets z = x**y mod |m| (x raised to y, modulo |m|). If m is nil, z = x**y.

big.Int.GCD.description: Sets z to the greatest common divisor of a and b. If x or y are not nil, GCD also sets x and y such that z = a*x + b*y.

big.NewFloat.description: Allocates and returns a new Float set to x with 53 bits of precision.

big.NewRat.description: Creates a new Rat with numerator a and denominator b.

big.Rat.description: Represents a quotient a/b of arbitrary precision. The zero value is a valid representation of the value 0.

smtp.SendMail.signature: func smtp.SendMail(addr string, a smtp.Auth, from string, to []string, msg []byte) error
smtp.SendMail.description: Connects to the server at addr, authenticates with mechanism a if possible, and sends an email from address from, to addresses to, with message msg.

smtp.PlainAuth.signature: func smtp.PlainAuth(identity, username, password, host string) smtp.Auth
smtp.PlainAuth.description: Returns an Auth that implements the PLAIN authentication mechanism. The returned Auth uses the given identity, username, password, and host.

smtp.NewClient.signature: func smtp.NewClient(conn net.Conn, host string) (*smtp.Client, error)
smtp.NewClient.description: Returns a new Client using an existing connection and host as the server name.

smtp.Dial.description: Returns a new Client connected to an SMTP server at addr.

mail.ParseAddress.description: Parses a single RFC 5322 address, e.g. 'Barry Gibbs <bg@example.com>'.

mail.ParseAddressList.description: Parses the given string as a list of addresses.

mail.Address.signature: type mail.Address struct { Name string; Address string }
mail.Address.description: Represents a single mail address. An address like 'Barry Gibbs <bg@example.com>' has Name='Barry Gibbs' and Address='bg@example.com'.

mail.ReadMessage.description: Reads a message from r. The headers are parsed, and the body of the message is available for reading from msg.Body.

mail.Message.signature: type mail.Message struct { Header mail.Header; Body io.Reader }

plugin.Open.description: Opens a Go plugin. If a path has already been opened, the existing *Plugin is returned. Linux and macOS only.

plugin.Plugin.Lookup.description: Searches for a symbol named symName in plugin p. A symbol is any exported variable or function.

plugin.Symbol.description: A plugin Symbol is a pointer to a variable or function. Use type assertions to access the actual value.

ast.File.signature: type ast.File struct { Package token.Pos; Name *ast.Ident; Decls []ast.Decl; Imports []*ast.ImportSpec; Comments []*ast.CommentGroup; ... }
ast.File.description: Represents a Go source file. Produced by go/parser.ParseFile.

ast.Ident.signature: type ast.Ident struct { NamePos token.Pos; Name string; Obj *ast.Object }
ast.Ident.description: Represents an identifier node in the AST.

ast.Inspect.signature: func ast.Inspect(node ast.Node, f func(ast.Node) bool)
ast.Inspect.description: Traverses an AST in depth-first order: it starts by calling f(node). If f returns true, Inspect invokes f recursively for each non-nil child of node.

ast.Walk.description: Traverses an AST in depth-first order: it calls v.Visit(node) for each node. If v.Visit returns a non-nil visitor w, Walk recursively walks with w.

ast.Node.signature: type ast.Node interface { Pos() token.Pos; End() token.Pos }
ast.Node.description: All node types implement the ast.Node interface.

ast.Visitor.signature: type ast.Visitor interface { Visit(node Node) (w Visitor) }
ast.Visitor.description: Interface for AST traversal with ast.Walk.

ast.Print.description: Prints x to standard output, skipping nil fields. A non-nil FieldFilter is called for struct fields.

ast.Fprint.signature: func ast.Fprint(w io.Writer, fset *token.FileSet, x any, f ast.FieldFilter) error
ast.Fprint.description: Prints the (sub-)tree starting at AST node x to w. If fset is not nil, position information is shown.

ast.FuncDecl.signature: type ast.FuncDecl struct { Doc *ast.CommentGroup; Recv *ast.FieldList; Name *ast.Ident; Type *ast.FuncType; Body *ast.BlockStmt }
ast.FuncDecl.description: Represents a function declaration node in the AST.

ast.GenDecl.signature: type ast.GenDecl struct { Doc *ast.CommentGroup; TokPos token.Pos; Tok token.Token; Lparen token.Pos; Specs []ast.Spec; Rparen token.Pos }
ast.GenDecl.description: Represents an import, constant, type or variable declaration (using token IMPORT, CONST, TYPE, VAR).

parser.ParseFile.signature: func parser.ParseFile(fset *token.FileSet, filename string, src any, mode parser.Mode) (*ast.File, error)
parser.ParseFile.description: Parses the source code of a single Go source file and returns the corresponding ast.File node. src can be string, []byte, io.Reader, or nil (to read from filename).

parser.ParseDir.signature: func parser.ParseDir(fset *token.FileSet, path string, filter func(os.FileInfo) bool, mode parser.Mode) (pkgs map[string]*ast.Package, first error)
parser.ParseDir.description: Calls ParseFile for all files with names ending in '.go' in the directory specified by path.

parser.ParseExpr.description: Parses the expression x as a Go expression.

parser.Mode.description: A set of flags that control the amount of source text the parser will process. Constants: PackageClauseOnly, ImportsOnly, ParseComments, Trace, DeclarationErrors, SpuriousErrors, SkipObjectResolution.

parser.ParseComments.description: Parse and retain documentation comments. If this flag is not set, documentation comments are dropped.

token.NewFileSet.description: Creates a new file set. Used by the parser to track file positions.

token.FileSet.description: Represents a set of source files. A FileSet is used to track each file's position information.

token.Pos.description: A compact encoding of a source position within a file set. Can be converted to a token.Position for human-readable information.

token.Position.signature: type token.Position struct { Filename string; Offset int; Line int; Column int }
token.Position.description: Describes a source position including file name, line, and column.

token.Token.description: The set of lexical tokens of the Go programming language. Constants include: IDENT, INT, FLOAT, STRING, FUNC, RETURN, etc.

token.FileSet.Position.description: Converts a Pos in the file set into a Position value.

format.Source.description: Formats Go source code. It parses src, adjusts the source code, and returns the formatted result. Like running gofmt.

format.Node.signature: func format.Node(dst io.Writer, fset *token.FileSet, node any) error
format.Node.description: Formats node in canonical gofmt style and writes the result to dst. The node type must be *ast.File, *printer.CommentedNode, []ast.Decl, []ast.Stmt, or an expression.

image.Decode.description: Decodes an image that has been encoded in a registered format. The string returned is the format name used during format registration.

image.DecodeConfig.description: Decodes the color model and dimensions of an image without decoding the entire image.

image.NewRGBA.description: Returns a new RGBA image with the given bounds.

image.NewNRGBA.description: Returns a new NRGBA (non-alpha-premultiplied) image with the given bounds.

image.Image.signature: type image.Image interface { ColorModel() color.Model; Bounds() Rectangle; At(x, y int) color.Color }
image.Image.description: A finite rectangular grid of color.Color values.

image.Rectangle.signature: type image.Rectangle struct { Min, Max image.Point }
image.Rectangle.description: An axis-aligned rectangle. Min is the top-left corner, Max is the bottom-right corner (exclusive).

image.Rect.signature: func image.Rect(x0, y0, x1, y1 int) image.Rectangle
image.Rect.description: Shortcut for image.Rectangle{Pt(x0, y0), Pt(x1, y1)}. The rectangle is canonicalized so Min is always <= Max.

image.Point.signature: type image.Point struct { X, Y int }
image.Point.description: An X, Y coordinate pair. The axes increase right and down.

png.Encode.signature: func png.Encode(w io.Writer, m image.Image) error
png.Encode.description: Writes the Image m to w in PNG format.

png.Decode.description: Reads a PNG image from r.

jpeg.Encode.signature: func jpeg.Encode(w io.Writer, m image.Image, o *jpeg.Options) error
jpeg.Encode.description: Writes the Image m to w in JPEG format. If o is nil, default quality (75) is used.

jpeg.Decode.description: Reads a JPEG image from r.

jpeg.Options.description: The encoding parameters for JPEG. Quality ranges from 1 to 100 inclusive, higher is better.

go:embed.description:
A compiler directive that embeds files and directories into the Go binary at compile time. Applied to a variable of type string, []byte, or embed.FS. Example: //go:embed hello.txt
var s string

go:generate.description: A compiler directive that identifies commands to run when 'go generate' is invoked. Runs the specified command in the package directory.

go:build.description: A build constraint (also known as a build tag) that identifies the conditions under which a file should be included in the package. Example: //go:build linux && amd64. Replaces the older // +build syntax.

go:linkname.description: A compiler directive that allows access to unexported symbols from another package. Used very rarely and only in the Go standard library internals.

go:noinline.description: A compiler directive that prevents the compiler from inlining the annotated function.

go:nosplit.description: A compiler directive that specifies the function must not include a stack overflow check. Used in low-level runtime code.

go:noescape.description: A compiler directive that specifies the function's arguments do not escape to the heap. Applied to body-less function declarations (assembly).

constraints.Ordered.description: A constraint that permits any ordered type: any type that supports the operators < <= >= >. From golang.org/x/exp/constraints (and used pattern in stdlib).

constraints.Integer.description: A constraint that permits any integer type.

constraints.Float.description: A constraint that permits any floating-point type.

constraints.Signed.description: A constraint that permits any signed integer type.

constraints.Unsigned.description: A constraint that permits any unsigned integer type.

runtime.LockOSThread.description: Wires the calling goroutine to its current operating system thread. The calling goroutine will always execute in that thread.

runtime.UnlockOSThread.description: Undoes an earlier call to LockOSThread.

math.MaxInt.description: Maximum value of an int (platform-dependent, 64-bit on 64-bit systems).

math.MaxFloat64.description: Largest finite value representable by float64.

math.SmallestNonzeroFloat64.description: Smallest positive non-zero value representable by float64.

sql.NullFloat64.signature: type sql.NullFloat64 struct { Float64 float64; Valid bool }
sql.NullFloat64.description: Represents a float64 that may be null.

sql.NullTime.signature: type sql.NullTime struct { Time time.Time; Valid bool }
sql.NullTime.description: Represents a time.Time that may be null.

sql.Named.signature: func sql.Named(name string, value any) sql.NamedArg
sql.Named.description: Creates a NamedArg with the given name and value. Use in query arguments: db.QueryRow('SELECT ... WHERE id = @id', sql.Named('id', 1)).

sql.Tx.Query.description: Executes a query that returns rows within the transaction.

sql.Tx.Exec.description: Executes a query that doesn't return rows within the transaction.

sql.Tx.QueryRow.description: Executes a query expected to return at most one row within the transaction.

sql.Tx.Prepare.description: Creates a prepared statement for use within a transaction.

log.Default.description: Returns the standard logger used by the package-level output functions.

log.Writer.description: Returns the output destination for the standard logger.

bufio.Scanner.Split.description: Sets the split function for the Scanner. Default is bufio.ScanLines. Must be called before Scan.

bufio.Scanner.Buffer.description: Sets the initial buffer to use when scanning and the maximum size of buffer that may be allocated during scanning. Must be called before Scan.

bufio.SplitFunc.signature: type bufio.SplitFunc func(data []byte, atEOF bool) (advance int, token []byte, err error)
bufio.SplitFunc.description: The signature of the split function used to tokenize the input.

nil.description: Predeclared identifier representing the zero value for pointers, interfaces, maps, slices, channels, and function types.

true.description: Boolean constant representing the truth value true.

false.description: Boolean constant representing the truth value false.

iota.description: Predeclared identifier representing the untyped integer ordinal number of the current const specification in a (possibly parenthesized) const declaration. It is zero-indexed and increments by one for each ConstSpec.

aes.NewCipher.description: Creates and returns a new AES cipher.Block. The key argument should be the AES key, 16, 24, or 32 bytes for AES-128, AES-192, and AES-256.

hmac.Equal.description: Compares two MACs for equality without leaking timing information (constant-time comparison).

hmac.New.signature: func hmac.New(h func() hash.Hash, key []byte) hash.Hash
hmac.New.description: Returns a new HMAC hash using the given hash function and key.

md5.New.description: Returns a new hash.Hash computing the MD5 checksum. MD5 is cryptographically broken; use SHA-256 for security.

md5.Sum.description: Returns the MD5 checksum of the data.

crypto_rand.Read.description: Reads len(b) bytes from the cryptographically secure random number generator. Always returns len(b) and a nil error.

sha256.New.description: Returns a new hash.Hash computing the SHA-256 checksum.

sha256.Sum256.description: Returns the SHA-256 checksum of the data.

sha512.New.description: Returns a new hash.Hash computing the SHA-512 checksum.

tls.Config.signature: type tls.Config struct { Certificates []Certificate; RootCAs *x509.CertPool; InsecureSkipVerify bool; MinVersion uint16; ... }
tls.Config.description: A TLS configuration. After creation, it can be passed to tls.Listen, tls.Dial, or http.Transport.

tls.Listen.signature: func tls.Listen(network, laddr string, config *tls.Config) (net.Listener, error)
tls.Listen.description: Creates a TLS listener accepting connections on the given network address.

x509.CertPool.description: A set of X.509 certificates. Use SystemCertPool() to get the system's certificate pool.

x509.SystemCertPool.description: Returns a copy of the system's certificate pool.

utf8.DecodeRuneInString.signature: func utf8.DecodeRuneInString(s string) (r rune, size int)
utf8.DecodeRuneInString.description: Unpacks the first UTF-8 encoding in s and returns the rune and its width in bytes.

utf8.RuneCountInString.description: Returns the number of runes in s. Erroneous and short encodings are treated as single runes of width 1 byte.

utf8.Valid.description: Reports whether p consists entirely of valid UTF-8-encoded runes.

utf8.ValidString.description: Reports whether s consists entirely of valid UTF-8-encoded runes.

crc32.ChecksumIEEE.description: Returns the CRC-32 checksum of data using the IEEE polynomial.

crc32.NewIEEE.description: Creates a new hash.Hash32 computing the CRC-32 checksum using the IEEE polynomial.

html_template.New.description: Creates a new HTML template. Auto-escapes HTML, JS, URLs for XSS safety.

html_template.Must.description: Wraps a template call and panics on error.

html_template.ParseFiles.description: Parses the named HTML template files.

html_template.ParseGlob.description: Parses all HTML template files matching the glob pattern.

html_template.HTMLEscapeString.description: Escapes special HTML characters in s.

iter.Seq.signature: type Seq[V any] func(yield func(V) bool)
iter.Seq.description: An iterator over sequences of individual values. The yield function is called for each value; iteration stops when yield returns false. Go 1.23+.

iter.Seq2.signature: type Seq2[K, V any] func(yield func(K, V) bool)
iter.Seq2.description: An iterator over sequences of key-value pairs. Go 1.23+.

iter.Pull.signature: func iter.Pull[V any](seq Seq[V]) (next func() (V, bool), stop func())
iter.Pull.description: Converts a push-style iterator into a pull-style iterator. Returns a next function and a stop function. Go 1.23+.

iter.Pull2.signature: func iter.Pull2[K, V any](seq Seq2[K, V]) (next func() (K, V, bool), stop func())
iter.Pull2.description: Converts a push-style Seq2 iterator into pull-style. Go 1.23+.

keyword.type.description: Declares a named type or type alias.

keyword.struct.description: Defines a composite type with named fields.

keyword.interface.description: Defines a set of method signatures. Types implicitly implement interfaces.

keyword.go.description: Starts a new goroutine running the function call.

keyword.defer.description: Schedules a function call to run when the surrounding function returns. LIFO order.

keyword.range.description: Iterates over elements of an array, slice, string, map, channel, or integer (Go 1.22+).

keyword.return.description: Terminates execution of the function and returns values to the caller.

keyword.break.description: Terminates execution of the innermost for, switch, or select statement.

keyword.continue.description: Begins the next iteration of the innermost for loop.

keyword.fallthrough.description: Transfers control to the next case in a switch statement.

keyword.goto.description: Transfers control to the labeled statement.

keyword.if.description: Conditional branch. Optional init statement separated by semicolon.

keyword.else.description: Alternative branch of an if statement.

keyword.for.description: The only looping construct in Go. Can be used as while loop (for condition {}), infinite loop (for {}), or range loop.

keyword.switch.description: Multi-way branch. Cases don't fall through by default.

keyword.case.description: A branch in a switch or select statement.

keyword.default.description: The default branch in a switch or select statement.

keyword.package.description: Declares the package name. Must be the first declaration in every Go source file.

keyword.import.description: Declares packages to import. Supports aliases, blank imports, and dot imports.

t.Run.signature: func (*testing.T) Run(name string, f func(t *testing.T)) bool
t.Run.description: Runs f as a subtest of t called name. It runs f in a separate goroutine and blocks until f returns or calls t.Parallel.

t.Error.description: Equivalent to Log followed by Fail. Marks the test as having failed but continues execution.

t.Errorf.description: Equivalent to Logf followed by Fail. Marks the test as failed and logs with formatting.

t.Fatal.description: Equivalent to Log followed by FailNow. Logs and stops the test immediately.

t.Fatalf.description: Equivalent to Logf followed by FailNow. Logs with formatting and stops the test immediately.

t.Log.description: Formats its arguments using default formatting and records the text in the error log. For tests, the text will be printed only if the test fails or the -test.v flag is set.

t.Logf.description: Formats its arguments according to the format like Sprintf and records the text in the error log.

t.Fail.description: Marks the test as failed but continues execution.

t.FailNow.description: Marks the test as failed and stops its execution. Does not stop other tests.

t.Failed.description: Reports whether the test has been marked as failed.

t.Skip.description: Equivalent to Log followed by SkipNow. Marks the test as skipped and stops its execution.

t.Skipf.description: Equivalent to Logf followed by SkipNow.

t.Helper.description: Marks the calling function as a test helper function. When printing file and line information, that function will be skipped. Helper may be called simultaneously from multiple goroutines.

t.Cleanup.description: Registers a function to be called when the test and all its subtests complete. Cleanup functions are called in LIFO order.

t.Parallel.description: Signals that this test is to be run in parallel with (and only with) other parallel tests. When a test is run in parallel, it will be paused until its parent test function has returned.

t.TempDir.description: Returns a temporary directory for the test to use. The directory is automatically removed when the test and all its subtests complete.

t.Name.description: Returns the name of the running test or benchmark.

t.Skipped.description: Reports whether the test was skipped.

t.Setenv.description: Calls os.Setenv(key, value) and uses Cleanup to restore the environment variable to its original value after the test. Cannot be used in parallel tests.

b.Run.signature: func (*testing.B) Run(name string, f func(b *testing.B)) bool
b.Run.description: Runs f as a sub-benchmark of b called name.

b.ReportAllocs.description: Enables malloc statistics for this benchmark. Equivalent to -test.benchmem.

b.ResetTimer.description: Zeroes the elapsed benchmark time and memory allocation counters and deletes user-reported metrics. Does not affect timer running state.

b.StartTimer.description: Starts timing a test. Called automatically before a benchmark starts, but can be used to resume timing after StopTimer.

b.StopTimer.description: Stops timing a test. Can be used to pause the timer while performing complex initialization.
